rows.title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly automate back-end knowledge work such as lesson planning, grading, and administrative communication, which currently occupy a large portion of a teacher's time. However, the core of the role involves physical supervision, emotional support, and real-time behavioral management of young children, which creates a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading can be digitized, the core value of the occupation is the live, in-person facilitation of child development, which requires physical presence and real-time social-emotional navigation. The classroom is a highly unpredictable, unstructured environment where the teacher acts as a moral and behavioral authority, making the human-to-human bond and professional liability non-delegatable to software agents.

rows.title: High school teachers
rows.old_rationale: High school teachers are heavily exposed because core digital tasks like lesson planning, content creation, and grading are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the physical requirement of classroom management and interpersonal mentorship provides a buffer, AI's ability to provide personalized tutoring and automated feedback significantly reshapes the instructional delivery and administrative workload of the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate lesson plans and grade assessments, the core value of teaching is rooted in human mentorship, emotional regulation, and the management of complex, unpredictable social dynamics within a classroom. The necessity for a human presence to provide moral authority, foster trust, and maintain accountability for student development makes the role highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Lawyers
rows.old_rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving document drafting, legal research, and complex data analysis—all areas where LLMs excel. While courtroom advocacy and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation provide a protective barrier, the vast majority of billable hours are spent on tasks that AI can now significantly accelerate or automate, leading to major structural changes in the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate legal documents and perform research (lowering the barrier for routine tasks), the core value of a lawyer lies in high-stakes advocacy, strategic judgment, and the ethical/legal necessity of human accountability. The profession is anchored by the requirement for a human to hold liability, provide moral authority, and navigate the interpersonal nuances of the courtroom and client counseling, which cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Middle school teachers
rows.old_rationale: Middle school teachers have high exposure because a significant portion of their workload—lesson planning, grading, and content creation—is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for classroom management, emotional support for adolescents, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading can be digitized, the core value of middle school teaching is rooted in human mentorship, emotional regulation, and the social development of adolescents, which cannot be substituted by an AI agent. The classroom environment is highly unpredictable, requiring real-time, high-stakes judgment and moral authority that necessitates a human presence for both pedagogical effectiveness and legal/ethical liability.

rows.title: Architectural and engineering managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and digital, involving project planning, budgeting, and technical oversight that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and physical site visits, the core tasks of analyzing technical data, scheduling, and coordinating complex engineering workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers oversee digital design processes, their core value lies in high-stakes decision-making, team leadership, and navigating the physical and regulatory constraints of construction and manufacturing. The requirement for professional liability, ethical oversight of safety-critical infrastructure, and the necessity of human trust in managing complex, multi-stakeholder projects makes them highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Natural sciences managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving data analysis, budgeting, and technical reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While the job requires significant human-centric leadership and physical oversight of laboratories, AI will drastically increase productivity in reviewing research, drafting operational reports, and optimizing resource allocation.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and reporting aspects of this role are increasingly digital, the core value lies in high-stakes scientific leadership, team motivation, and the ethical oversight of research, which requires human judgment and accountability. The role involves navigating complex, unpredictable physical environments and human dynamics that cannot be fully captured or executed by autonomous agents.

rows.title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital tasks (writing case reports, risk assessment, and data-driven monitoring) and low-exposure physical/interpersonal tasks (home visits, drug testing, and building trust with hostile clients). While AI can significantly automate documentation and predictive risk modeling, the legal requirement for human testimony and the physical necessity of community supervision provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with reporting and risk assessment, the core value of this role lies in physical presence, de-escalation in volatile environments, and the exercise of human judgment in high-stakes legal and ethical contexts. The occupation requires non-delegatable legal authority and the establishment of human trust, making it highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Judges and hearing officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of researching legal issues, evaluating documents, and writing opinions are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, which can significantly increase productivity and reduce the need for human staff. However, the role requires high-stakes human judgment, presiding over physical courtrooms, and maintaining public trust, which creates a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in legal research and document drafting, the core output of a judge—a binding legal ruling—requires human moral authority, social legitimacy, and the assumption of life-altering liability that cannot be delegated to software. The judicial process is fundamentally rooted in the necessity of a human arbiter to ensure fairness, interpret intent, and maintain public trust in the rule of law, rendering the agentic potential extremely low despite the high cognitive complexity of the work.

rows.title: Air traffic controllers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving the monitoring of radar screens and the processing of complex spatial information to ensure aircraft separation. While the high-stakes nature of safety and the need for real-time human communication with pilots provide a buffer, AI and automated systems (like NextGen) are already increasing productivity and handling more traffic per controller, leading to a very low projected growth in human employment.
rows.new_rationale: While air traffic control involves digital data, the output is a high-stakes, real-time intervention in a physical environment where errors result in immediate loss of life, limiting the 'agentic' potential for fully autonomous replacement. The necessity for human accountability and moral authority in life-or-death decision-making remains absolute, as society currently requires a human to bear the legal and ethical burden of managing complex, unpredictable variables in the national airspace.

rows.title: Nuclear engineers
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear engineering is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—such as reactor design, dose calculations, and safety simulations—are performed using digital tools and complex mathematical modeling. While the high-stakes regulatory environment and the need for physical inspections at power plants provide a buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in its ability to optimize designs, analyze sensor data for predictive maintenance, and automate technical documentation.
rows.new_rationale: While nuclear engineers produce technical designs and reports that are digital, the high-stakes physical nature of nuclear energy means these outputs are merely inputs for physical systems that require human validation and oversight. The extreme safety requirements and the catastrophic potential of errors create a non-delegatable accountability burden that necessitates human moral and legal authority, rendering AI-only solutions insufficient for the core value of the role.

rows.title: Emergency management directors
rows.old_rationale: The role involves significant digital knowledge work, such as analyzing hazards, drafting complex response plans, and managing federal funding applications, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the job requires high-stakes real-time decision-making, physical presence at command centers during disasters, and intense interpersonal coordination with government officials and the public, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and planning, the core value of this role lies in high-stakes, real-time leadership during chaotic, unpredictable physical disasters where human judgment and moral authority are essential. The role requires navigating complex political and social dynamics and carries profound legal and ethical accountability for life-altering decisions that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of legal documents, evidence, and the drafting of settlement agreements—tasks where AI is rapidly achieving high proficiency. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence to manage confrontational parties, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and partial automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the final settlement agreement is a digital document, the core value of this role is the human-led process of de-escalation, empathy, and building trust between adversarial parties, which AI cannot authentically replicate. The high scores in accountability and human necessity reflect the fact that parties submit to these processes specifically because they require a human arbiter to provide moral and social legitimacy to the resolution of high-stakes conflicts.

rows.title: Retail sales workers
rows.old_rationale: Retail sales involve a mix of physical tasks (stocking shelves, demonstrating products) and interpersonal interaction that provides a barrier to full automation. However, AI is increasingly handling the information-heavy aspects of the job, such as personalized product recommendations, answering technical questions via kiosks/mobile apps, and automating inventory management and checkout processes, which reduces the total human labor required per store.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of retail sales—product recommendation, inventory management, and transaction processing—is increasingly being offloaded to digital interfaces and AI-driven kiosks, which consumers already accept as substitutes. While the physical environment of a store requires some human presence for stocking and security, the actual 'sales' output is highly susceptible to automation, balanced only by a moderate need for human interpersonal engagement in high-touch or luxury retail contexts.

rows.title: Top executives
rows.old_rationale: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize data for decision support, the core output of a top executive is leadership, moral authority, and the assumption of legal/fiduciary liability, which cannot be delegated to software. The role requires navigating highly unpredictable, human-centric political and social environments where the 'human-in-the-loop' is not just a preference but a requirement for institutional trust and accountability.

rows.title: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rows.old_rationale: While AI and autonomous vehicle technology are advancing, the core of this job involves significant physical labor, such as loading/unloading cargo and navigating unpredictable 'last-mile' environments. AI may optimize routing and assist with sales communication, but the physical requirement to move goods from a vehicle to a customer's door remains a major barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical act of loading and navigating unstructured urban environments remains a hurdle for current robotics, the core value of the job—transporting goods from point A to point B—is highly susceptible to autonomous agent substitution as vehicle automation matures. The cognitive requirements are relatively low, and the human-to-human interaction is largely functional, meaning the market would readily accept an automated delivery system as a direct replacement for a human driver.

rows.title: Physicians and surgeons
rows.old_rationale: Physicians and surgeons have moderate exposure because while AI can significantly automate digital tasks like diagnostic image interpretation (radiology), medical history analysis, and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical presence, manual dexterity for procedures, and complex interpersonal communication. The high-stakes nature of medical liability and the need for human empathy in patient care provide a natural barrier to full automation, though AI will drastically increase productivity in information-heavy specialties.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostics and data synthesis, the core output of a physician—physical intervention, patient trust, and ethical stewardship of human life—remains fundamentally tied to human presence and physical action. The high stakes of medical outcomes in unpredictable, real-world biological environments necessitate human accountability and empathy that cannot be substituted by digital agents.

rows.title: Police and detectives
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical presence, emergency response, and manual tasks like making arrests and patrolling, which are resistant to AI. However, a significant portion of the work involves digital tasks such as writing detailed reports, searching databases, and analyzing evidence, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in physical presence, the exercise of state-sanctioned authority, and the moral/legal responsibility for life-altering decisions, which cannot be delegated to software. While AI can assist with data analysis and report generation, the primary output—maintaining public order, apprehending suspects, and navigating volatile, unstructured physical environments—requires human agency, physical intervention, and ethical accountability that society currently demands be performed by a human.

rows.title: Special education teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the digital and administrative aspects of the role, such as drafting Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), adapting lesson plans, and tracking student data. However, the core of the job requires intense physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time behavioral management for students with complex disabilities, which AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in generating lesson plans or tracking data, the core value of special education is the human-to-human pedagogical bond, emotional regulation, and physical presence required to support students with diverse disabilities. The work environment is highly unpredictable, requiring real-time, empathetic, and physical intervention that cannot be delegated to software, and the legal and ethical accountability for the development and well-being of vulnerable students remains strictly human.

rows.title: Construction managers
rows.old_rationale: Construction management is a hybrid role that combines digital knowledge work—such as cost estimation, scheduling, and contract analysis—with essential physical presence. While AI can significantly automate budgeting and logistical planning, the core of the job requires real-world site inspections, physical problem-solving, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with tradesworkers and inspectors that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: While construction managers use software for scheduling and budgeting, their core value lies in managing physical, high-stakes, and highly unpredictable environments where real-time human judgment is required to navigate site-specific crises. The role requires deep accountability for safety, legal compliance, and the coordination of human labor, making it resistant to full agentic substitution despite the availability of digital planning tools.

rows.title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-level knowledge work and intense interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like documentation, treatment planning, and monitoring progress, the core of the job requires human empathy, crisis intervention, and the navigation of complex emotional and physical environments that AI cannot yet replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with documentation and basic behavioral coaching, the core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human therapeutic alliance, empathy, and the ethical weight of managing life-altering crises. The high environmental unpredictability—ranging from correctional facilities to crisis intervention—combined with the necessity for human presence and legal accountability, makes this occupation highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of high-level knowledge work, such as diagnosing conditions and analyzing test results, which is highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for exams, procedures (like delivering babies or administering anesthesia), and complex interpersonal communication that provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, real-time management of human life, which requires physical presence, tactile intervention, and high-stakes, non-delegatable accountability. While AI can assist with diagnostics and data synthesis, the necessity for human judgment in volatile, life-critical clinical environments and the legal requirement for human liability make the agentic output potential extremely low.

rows.title: Civil engineers
rows.old_rationale: Civil engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, including CAD design, data analysis, and cost estimation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role is anchored by the necessity for physical site inspections, real-world surveying, and the legal accountability of a Professional Engineer (PE) license, which requires human judgment and liability that AI cannot currently replace.
rows.new_rationale: While civil engineers use digital design tools, their core value lies in physical infrastructure integration, which requires navigating highly unpredictable, site-specific environmental variables that software cannot currently resolve autonomously. Furthermore, the role carries immense non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for public safety, necessitating a human 'Professional Engineer' to sign off on designs and assume responsibility for life-altering outcomes.

rows.title: Elementary, middle, and high school principals
rows.old_rationale: Principals perform significant knowledge work—such as curriculum development, budget management, and data analysis of student performance—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. However, the core of the role requires physical presence for school safety, real-time crisis management, and high-stakes interpersonal interactions with students, parents, and staff that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and data reporting have some digital output potential, the core of the principal's role involves high-stakes human mediation, crisis management in unpredictable social environments, and moral/legal accountability for student welfare. The necessity for a human presence to provide leadership, empathy, and institutional trust makes the role highly resistant to substitution by autonomous agents.

rows.title: Mechanical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mechanical engineering is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD, simulations, and data analysis, all of which are being rapidly enhanced by AI to automate complex design iterations and predictive modeling. While the role requires physical prototyping, site visits, and hardware testing, the core intellectual work of designing and analyzing mechanical systems is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While mechanical engineers produce digital CAD models and simulations, the final value is a physical, high-stakes machine that must operate safely in the real world, limiting the 'agentic' substitution of the entire lifecycle. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical necessity for a licensed professional to sign off on designs that, if failed, result in catastrophic physical harm, a responsibility that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Industrial production managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical oversight. AI will significantly enhance the analytical aspects of the job, such as production scheduling, budget optimization, and predictive maintenance data analysis, but the core requirements of physical plant presence, personnel management, and real-time problem-solving in a manual environment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While production data analysis can be automated, the core value of this role lies in managing physical, high-stakes, and often unpredictable manufacturing environments where human judgment is required for safety, labor relations, and crisis resolution. The necessity for human leadership, on-site troubleshooting of complex mechanical/logistical failures, and ultimate legal/ethical accountability for plant operations makes full agentic substitution highly improbable.

rows.title: Human resources managers
rows.old_rationale: Human resources management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation where AI can significantly automate recruitment screening, payroll processing, and regulatory compliance reporting. While the role requires high-level interpersonal skills for dispute mediation and strategic leadership, the vast majority of the administrative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and data-driven work of HR can be digitized, the core value of the role lies in high-stakes interpersonal mediation, ethical judgment, and the navigation of complex human dynamics that require a human presence for legitimacy. The necessity for human accountability in legal, disciplinary, and sensitive employee-relations matters creates a high barrier to full agentic substitution, as the 'human-to-human' bond is central to the function.

rows.title: Social and community service managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as grant writing, data analysis, and budget management, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core responsibilities of community stakeholder engagement, staff supervision, and navigating complex human crises provide a buffer that requires physical presence and high-level emotional intelligence.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like data reporting and proposal writing have high agentic potential, the core of this role involves navigating complex, unpredictable human crises and community dynamics that require authentic human presence. The necessity for moral authority, trust-based leadership, and accountability for the well-being of vulnerable populations makes the human element non-substitutable, despite the availability of AI for analytical support.

rows.title: Psychologists
rows.old_rationale: Psychology is a knowledge-based profession where core tasks like analyzing data, writing reports, and researching are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the critical requirements for human empathy, ethical judgment, and real-time interpersonal observation in clinical and school settings create a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostic data and pattern recognition, the core value of psychology is rooted in the therapeutic alliance, empathy, and the human-to-human connection, which cannot be substituted by a digital agent. The high stakes of mental health treatment and the legal/ethical requirements for licensure and accountability necessitate a human practitioner, rendering the agentic output potential very low despite the cognitive complexity of the work.

rows.title: Producers and directors
rows.old_rationale: Producers and directors are highly exposed because their core work products—scripts, visual aesthetics, and edited video—are increasingly being generated or enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires significant human leadership and physical presence on set, the rapid advancement of generative AI in pre-production (scripting/storyboarding) and post-production (editing/VFX) allows a single director to do the work of a much larger team, potentially consolidating the industry.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly assist in post-production and digital asset generation, the core value of a producer or director lies in human leadership, the orchestration of complex physical sets, and the curation of artistic 'vision' that audiences currently demand from a human creator. The role requires navigating highly unpredictable, high-stakes physical environments and managing interpersonal dynamics that are central to the product's authenticity and success.

rows.title: Medical scientists
rows.old_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can accelerate data analysis and hypothesis generation, the core value of a medical scientist lies in the physical execution of novel experiments, the ethical oversight of clinical trials, and the high-stakes accountability for human health outcomes. The necessity for human judgment in interpreting ambiguous biological data and the legal/ethical burden of medical research ensure that the role remains fundamentally human-centric, despite the increasing utility of digital tools.

rows.title: Airline and commercial pilots
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-level digital information processing and critical physical presence. While AI and advanced automation can handle navigation, flight planning, and system monitoring, the role requires real-time human intervention for physical emergencies, complex sensory awareness in unpredictable weather, and the legal/ethical responsibility of passenger safety in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: While flight systems are increasingly automated, the core output is the physical transport of human lives in a highly volatile, unstructured environment where real-time, high-stakes physical intervention is required. The necessity for a human pilot is driven by the requirement for moral and legal accountability in life-or-death decision-making, which cannot be offloaded to software regardless of technical capability.

rows.title: Architects
rows.old_rationale: Architects perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, utilizing CADD and BIM software which is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation in generative design and technical documentation. While the role requires physical site visits and complex client negotiations, a significant portion of the core work—drafting, specification writing, and regulatory compliance—is moving toward high AI integration.
rows.new_rationale: While BIM and AI can generate structural models, the architect's role involves high-stakes synthesis of aesthetic, regulatory, and environmental variables that require human judgment. The necessity for physical site visits to manage unpredictable construction realities and the absolute requirement for a licensed human to hold legal liability for public safety make full automation impossible.

rows.title: Chemists and materials scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a high-level blend of digital knowledge work and physical laboratory experimentation. AI is already revolutionizing the digital aspects—such as molecular modeling, predictive simulation, and technical reporting—allowing scientists to screen millions of compounds in silico before ever entering a lab. While the physical requirement of conducting experiments and handling chemicals provides a buffer, the massive productivity gains in research and analysis mean AI will fundamentally restructure how these scientists spend their time.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with molecular modeling and data analysis, the core value of this profession lies in the physical manipulation of matter, experimental validation in non-deterministic laboratory environments, and the synthesis of novel materials that do not yet exist in digital space. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical safety, legal, and ethical implications of chemical and pharmaceutical development, where human oversight remains a non-delegatable requirement for liability and risk management.

rows.title: Marriage and family therapists
rows.old_rationale: While the work is increasingly digital via telehealth and involves significant information processing, the core value of therapy lies in human-to-human empathy, trust, and the navigation of complex interpersonal dynamics. AI will likely automate administrative tasks and provide diagnostic support, but the high-stakes emotional nature of family and marriage counseling provides a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this profession is rooted in the human-to-human therapeutic alliance, where empathy, trust, and shared lived experience are the primary mechanisms of change, rendering AI substitution fundamentally inadequate. While AI can assist with documentation or data-driven insights, the high-stakes nature of mental health, legal liability, and the requirement for authentic human connection make the occupation highly resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Interpreters and translators
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is purely digital information (text and speech) in a domain where Large Language Models already demonstrate near-human proficiency. While high-stakes interpreting (legal, medical) and sign language still require human presence and nuanced judgment, the vast majority of translation and routine interpreting tasks are being rapidly automated or shifted to 'post-editing' roles, significantly reducing the total human labor required per unit of work.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of translation and interpretation is digital or auditory information, which is highly susceptible to AI substitution as machine translation and real-time speech-to-speech models mature. While high-stakes environments like courtrooms or medical settings require human accountability and cultural nuance, the fundamental task of linguistic conversion is increasingly being offloaded to software, limiting the long-term necessity for human intervention in routine contexts.

rows.title: Aerospace engineers
rows.old_rationale: Aerospace engineering is a high-level knowledge occupation where core tasks like aerodynamic modeling, structural analysis, and propulsion design are increasingly performed using digital simulation and AI-driven optimization tools. While the role requires critical human judgment for safety, regulatory compliance, and physical inspection of prototypes, the heavy reliance on computer-aided design (CAD) and complex data analysis makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task automation.
rows.new_rationale: While aerospace engineers produce digital design artifacts, the high-stakes nature of physical flight safety and the requirement for professional licensure and ethical liability mean that AI cannot 'sign off' on critical systems. The work involves navigating complex, non-routine physical variables and regulatory environments where human judgment and legal accountability remain non-delegatable requirements for the final product.

rows.title: Labor relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the drafting of contracts, policy development, and legal compliance—all areas where AI excels. While the high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and dispute resolution provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document generation, and investigative analysis components of the role.
rows.new_rationale: While the drafting of contracts and policies is a digital output, the core value of this role lies in high-stakes mediation, conflict resolution, and the navigation of human emotions and power dynamics, which require a human presence to be perceived as legitimate. The necessity for trust, moral authority, and the ability to manage volatile interpersonal disputes makes the role highly resistant to full agentic substitution, despite the availability of AI for document generation and data analysis.

rows.title: Sales engineers
rows.old_rationale: Sales engineers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including technical presentations, product customization, and troubleshooting. While AI can automate technical documentation, demo generation, and data-driven customer insights, the role's heavy reliance on complex interpersonal relationships, negotiation, and trust-building provides a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the technical documentation and product configuration aspects of the role have high agentic potential, the core value of a Sales Engineer is rooted in high-stakes persuasion, trust-building, and navigating complex human organizational dynamics. Because the 'product' is fundamentally a human-to-human relationship that secures high-value contracts, AI cannot replace the ontological necessity of a human agent to provide accountability and authentic interpersonal influence.

rows.title: Urban and regional planners
rows.old_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
rows.new_rationale: While urban planners utilize digital tools like GIS and data modeling, their core value lies in navigating complex, high-stakes political and social trade-offs that require human consensus-building and moral authority. The 'output' is not merely a digital plan, but a socially negotiated agreement that must withstand public scrutiny and legal accountability, making full automation of the decision-making process unlikely.

rows.title: Private detectives and investigators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of digital research and physical field work. AI significantly enhances the digital aspects—such as background checks, social media analysis, and report writing—making investigators much more productive, but the core requirements of physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and undercover store work provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital research and data synthesis are increasingly automated, the core value of this role relies on physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and the navigation of unpredictable, unstructured real-world environments. Furthermore, the necessity for human judgment in legal compliance, the ethical weight of investigative findings, and the requirement for a human presence to establish trust or conduct undercover operations creates a high barrier to full AI substitution.

rows.title: Physicists and astronomers
rows.old_rationale: Physicists and astronomers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including data analysis, mathematical modeling, and software development. While AI significantly accelerates data processing and literature review, the core requirements of original theoretical innovation, experimental design, and complex physical-world problem solving provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and modeling, the core value of this profession lies in the creation of novel physical experiments, the design of bespoke scientific hardware, and the interpretation of anomalous data in unstructured or high-stakes physical environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in fields like medical physics, nuclear energy, and national security, where human judgment remains the final arbiter of safety and validity.

rows.title: Bioengineers and biomedical engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves heavy digital knowledge work, including software design, statistical modeling, and technical writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical tasks like equipment maintenance and clinical collaboration provide a buffer, the core engineering and research functions are increasingly driven by AI-enhanced simulations and data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in modeling and simulation, the core output of this role involves physical medical devices and biological interventions that require rigorous safety validation and physical integration with human physiology. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human judgment in navigating the unpredictable, high-stakes nature of clinical environments and biological systems make full agentic substitution unlikely.

rows.title: Landscape architects
rows.old_rationale: Landscape architecture is a predominantly digital knowledge-based occupation where core tasks like CADD modeling, site analysis, and cost estimation are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires physical site visits and interpersonal client management, the generative design capabilities of AI can significantly accelerate the creative and technical drafting phases, potentially reducing the total human hours required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and modeling phases are increasingly digital, the core value of landscape architecture lies in navigating highly variable, unstructured physical environments where site-specific constraints (soil, drainage, local ecology) require real-world judgment. The occupation carries significant systemic accountability for public safety, environmental impact, and regulatory compliance, necessitating a human professional to sign off on designs and manage the physical reality of construction, which AI cannot yet fully substitute.

rows.title: Forensic science technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical evidence collection and digital analysis. While AI can significantly automate pattern recognition (fingerprints, ballistics) and statistical reporting, the job requires physical presence at crime scenes to collect samples and the human credibility necessary to testify in court as an expert witness.
rows.new_rationale: While laboratory analysis involves digital reporting, the core value of the role is rooted in the physical collection of evidence in unstructured, high-stakes crime scenes and the maintenance of a legally defensible chain of custody. The requirement for human testimony in court and the absolute necessity of human accountability for life-altering legal outcomes make this role highly resistant to full AI substitution, despite the increasing use of AI in data processing.

rows.title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital design, complex mathematical modeling, and technical report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation. While physical site visits to shipyards and offshore platforms provide a buffer, the shift toward AI-integrated CAD, automated performance testing, and generative design for hulls and machinery significantly reshapes the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: While design schematics are digital, the final output is a physical, high-stakes vessel or structure that must operate in volatile marine environments, limiting the 'agentic' substitution of the physical build and safety validation. The high systemic accountability and the need for professional licensure to sign off on life-critical engineering decisions create a significant barrier to full AI autonomy, as the human must remain the legal and ethical anchor for safety-critical failures.

rows.title: Mining and geological engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mining and geological engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including mine design, technical reporting, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role also requires substantial physical presence for site evaluations, safety inspections, and supervising construction in remote, unpredictable environments, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While design and planning tasks are increasingly digital, the core value of this role is tied to physical, high-stakes operations in volatile, unstructured geological environments where AI cannot yet navigate or physically intervene. The high systemic accountability score reflects the non-delegatable legal and ethical burden of ensuring worker safety and environmental compliance in life-critical mining operations.

rows.title: Military careers
rows.old_rationale: Military careers are a vast composite of physical combat, manual labor, and high-level digital strategy. While AI will heavily automate back-office administration, logistics, and intelligence analysis (high exposure), the core requirements of physical presence, tactical human leadership, and operation in unpredictable physical environments provide a significant buffer.
rows.new_rationale: Military service is fundamentally defined by physical presence in high-stakes, unstructured, and volatile environments where the core output is the exercise of force, protection, or strategic presence, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. The necessity for human moral judgment, legal liability in the use of force, and the requirement for physical courage and leadership makes the occupation highly resistant to AI substitution.

rows.title: Registered nurses
rows.old_rationale: Registered nursing is a hybrid role that combines significant physical labor, such as administering treatments and moving patients, with complex interpersonal care and emotional support. While AI will heavily impact the knowledge-work components—such as patient monitoring, diagnostic analysis, and care planning—the core requirement for physical presence and human empathy creates a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Nursing is fundamentally defined by physical, high-stakes interaction with human bodies in unpredictable clinical environments, rendering the core value non-agentic. While AI can assist with data synthesis, the requirement for real-time physical intervention, emotional labor, and non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for patient life makes human presence an ontological necessity rather than a replaceable digital function.

rows.title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is physical and occurs in a complex real-world environment, it is a primary target for autonomous vehicle AI, which could eventually automate long-haul highway segments. However, the job still requires significant physical presence for non-driving tasks like securing cargo, conducting inspections, and navigating 'last-mile' urban environments that remain challenging for current AI.
rows.new_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is actively targeting this sector, the core output remains a physical, real-world service (transporting heavy cargo) that requires navigating highly unstructured and volatile environments. The high score for systemic accountability reflects the life-altering consequences of operating 26,000-pound vehicles, where legal and ethical liability for safety remains a significant barrier to full, unmonitored delegation to software.

rows.title: Accountants and auditors
rows.old_rationale: Accountancy is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on data analysis, regulatory compliance, and reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI and robotic process automation. While high-level advisory roles and complex forensic audits require human judgment and relationship management, the core tasks of data organization, tax computation, and anomaly detection are increasingly being automated, significantly increasing worker productivity and restructuring the entry-level labor market.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of accounting—financial statements, tax filings, and audit reports—is inherently digital and increasingly generated by software, giving it high agentic potential. However, the profession remains anchored by systemic accountability (legal liability for SEC filings and audits) and the ontological necessity of human trust, professional judgment, and ethical certification, which prevents full automation despite the high technical feasibility of the underlying data processing.

rows.title: Teacher assistants
rows.old_rationale: Teacher assistants have moderate exposure because AI can significantly automate their administrative and instructional support tasks, such as grading, lesson preparation, and providing personalized tutoring through AI agents. However, a large portion of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and assisting students with special needs or personal hygiene, which require a physical human presence that AI cannot replace.
rows.new_rationale: While some administrative tasks are digitizable, the core value of a teacher assistant lies in physical supervision, emotional support, and hands-on assistance for students with special needs, which cannot be replaced by software. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the nature of child behavior and the physical requirements of classroom management, necessitating a human presence for safety, trust, and ethical liability.

rows.title: Postsecondary teachers
rows.old_rationale: Postsecondary teaching is primarily knowledge work involving research, writing, and curriculum design—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human interaction and mentorship, AI can automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews, potentially increasing individual productivity to the point of restructuring faculty needs.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the informational content of teaching can be digitized, the core value of postsecondary education remains deeply rooted in mentorship, human-to-human intellectual engagement, and the social legitimacy of the credentialing process. The high ontological necessity score reflects that students and institutions prioritize the human connection and moral authority of the educator, which cannot be fully substituted by an AI agent despite the high cognitive complexity of the research and instructional design involved.

rows.title: Project management specialists
rows.old_rationale: Project management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation involving data analysis, scheduling, and documentation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While AI can significantly increase productivity in planning and monitoring, the role's heavy reliance on human-centric skills like stakeholder negotiation, conflict resolution, and team leadership provides a protective barrier against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While project management involves significant digital artifact creation (plans, schedules, reports) that AI can increasingly generate, the core value relies on human-to-human negotiation, conflict resolution, and building trust with stakeholders. The role requires high-level synthesis of disparate human and technical variables, and the accountability for project failure—often involving significant financial or operational risk—remains a deeply human responsibility that organizations are unlikely to fully delegate to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Food preparation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable kitchen environments, which are difficult for AI to automate directly. While AI might optimize inventory management or scheduling, the primary tasks of washing, cutting, and preparing food remain resistant to digital replacement, though some labor may be displaced by industrial food processing automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the tasks are highly procedural, the output is physical and requires interaction with unstructured, dynamic kitchen environments (slippery floors, hot surfaces, varying ingredient states) that remain difficult for current autonomous agents to navigate reliably. The low agentic potential score reflects that the core value is physical labor—chopping, cleaning, and lifting—which cannot be delivered as a digital artifact or software command.

rows.title: Social workers
rows.old_rationale: Social work involves a high degree of interpersonal interaction, empathy, and crisis management in physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against full automation. However, AI will significantly impact the profession by automating administrative tasks like case documentation, assisting in resource research, and providing diagnostic support tools for clinical practitioners.
rows.new_rationale: Social work is fundamentally rooted in human-to-human connection, empathy, and the physical presence required to navigate volatile, high-stakes environments like crisis intervention or child welfare. While AI can assist with documentation or resource matching, the core value—trust, moral authority, and the ethical responsibility for life-altering decisions—cannot be delegated to a machine, as the market and legal systems require human accountability for these sensitive interventions.

rows.title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, dressing, and wound care, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. However, AI will significantly impact the information-processing aspects of the job, including documenting patient care, monitoring vitals, and assisting with diagnostic reporting, leading to moderate overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, bedside care and the human-to-human bond, which cannot be substituted by digital artifacts or software agents. While the job involves monitoring and documentation, the primary tasks—physical assistance, patient comfort, and real-time clinical judgment in unpredictable, high-stakes environments—require a human presence that is legally and ethically non-delegatable.

rows.title: Sales managers
rows.old_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and forecasting can be automated, the core value of a sales manager lies in human-to-human leadership, motivation, and the navigation of complex, trust-based professional relationships. The role requires high-level synthesis of market dynamics and human psychology, and the necessity for a human to provide accountability and authentic interpersonal influence remains a significant barrier to full AI substitution.

rows.title: Medical and health services managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a high volume of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core responsibilities of recruiting, supervising staff, and navigating complex interpersonal relationships within a physical healthcare facility provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative data processing and scheduling can be automated, the core value of this role lies in high-stakes personnel management, regulatory compliance, and navigating the complex, human-centric dynamics of healthcare facilities. The necessity for human leadership, ethical judgment in patient-care environments, and legal accountability for facility operations makes full substitution by an AI agent highly unlikely, despite the digital nature of many individual tasks.

rows.title: Quality control inspectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical inspection and data analysis. While AI-powered computer vision systems are rapidly automating the visual detection of defects, the role still requires significant physical presence to handle materials, operate manual gauges, and perform mechanical tests that robots cannot yet fully replicate in diverse manufacturing environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—the binary decision to accept or reject a product based on measurement data—is highly susceptible to automation via computer vision and sensor-integrated software. While the physical environment can be noisy or hazardous, the task itself is procedural and data-driven, requiring little human-specific empathy or complex, non-routine synthesis that an AI agent could not replicate or exceed.

rows.title: Bus drivers
rows.old_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is a direct long-term threat to the core task of vehicle operation, the role involves significant physical and interpersonal responsibilities that AI cannot yet replicate. School bus drivers must manage student behavior and safety, while transit and charter drivers assist passengers with disabilities and handle physical luggage, creating a substantial barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is advancing, the core value of the job remains the physical, real-time navigation of unstructured, high-stakes environments (traffic, weather, pedestrians). The high score for ontological necessity and accountability reflects the critical human role in ensuring passenger safety, managing unruly behavior, and providing care for children and passengers with disabilities, which cannot be delegated to software in a way that satisfies public trust or legal liability.

rows.title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
rows.old_rationale: Real estate agents perform a mix of digital knowledge work and physical/interpersonal tasks. AI can automate significant portions of their digital workload, such as market analysis, property descriptions, lead generation, and contract preparation, which increases individual productivity and may reduce the total number of agents needed. However, the core of the job still requires physical property showings, high-stakes human negotiation, and local networking, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data management and listing process can be digitized, the core value of the role relies on high-stakes interpersonal negotiation, emotional labor, and the physical act of property assessment and showing. The requirement for a human to act as a trusted fiduciary and mediator in a high-value, emotionally charged transaction creates a strong barrier to full AI substitution, despite the potential for software to handle the administrative and informational components.

rows.title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—executing trades, managing portfolios, and structuring financial deals—is increasingly digital and software-executable, leading to high agentic potential. However, the role retains high ontological necessity and accountability scores because clients require human trust, fiduciary responsibility, and legal liability for high-stakes, life-altering financial decisions that cannot be fully offloaded to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Pharmacy technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as measuring medications, packaging prescriptions, and interacting with patients in person, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI and automated dispensing systems are highly capable of handling the information-processing tasks like insurance claims, inventory management, and prescription data entry, which constitute a large portion of the daily workload.
rows.new_rationale: A significant portion of the pharmacy technician's workflow involves data entry, insurance processing, and inventory management, which are highly susceptible to end-to-end software automation. While the physical act of dispensing and the requirement for human oversight in a clinical setting provide a buffer, the core output—the preparation and verification of medication orders—is increasingly being handled by automated dispensing systems and digital workflows, limiting the long-term necessity for human intervention in the mechanical aspects of the role.

rows.title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate significant portions of the job—such as drafting leases, financial reporting, budgeting, and coordinating maintenance requests—the role still requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution. The exposure is high because the administrative and analytical 'office' tasks that comprise a large part of the day are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like financial reporting and scheduling are highly automatable, the core value of this role relies on physical presence, conflict resolution, and the human-to-human trust required to manage residential and community environments. The necessity for onsite inspections, physical mediation of disputes, and the legal/ethical accountability of managing real-world property assets creates a significant barrier to full AI substitution.

rows.title: Social and human service assistants
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital administrative work and high-touch interpersonal service. AI can significantly automate the 'knowledge work' components, such as researching community resources, assisting with complex benefit applications, and coordinating service schedules. However, the core of the job requires physical presence in homes or clinics and deep emotional intelligence to support clients in crisis, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and research components of this role (finding resources, filing paperwork) have high digital potential, the core value is rooted in high-stakes, in-person human interaction, crisis management, and the provision of emotional support. The work environment is highly unpredictable, involving vulnerable populations in volatile situations where the presence of a trusted, empathetic human is an ontological requirement for the service to be effective and ethically sound.

rows.title: Financial analysts
rows.old_rationale: Financial analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the processing of large datasets, trend analysis, and the generation of reports—all areas where AI excels. While high-level strategy and client relationships provide some insulation, the core tasks of data evaluation, risk assessment, and financial modeling are being rapidly automated or significantly enhanced by AI, leading to major restructuring of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a financial analyst—investment recommendations, risk assessments, and financial reports—is entirely digital and can be executed directly by software agents, leading to a very high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex market variables, the environment is highly controlled and data-centric; however, the role retains a significant human necessity component due to the requirement for interpersonal trust, client relationship management, and the legal/ethical accountability inherent in managing large-scale financial assets.

rows.title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are physical and interpersonal, requiring real-time human presence to maintain security, conduct searches, and physically restrain individuals. AI exposure is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like report writing and log entry, or enhancing surveillance systems, but it cannot replace the physical oversight and intervention required in correctional and courtroom environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical presence, the exercise of force, and the maintenance of order in highly volatile, unstructured environments where human judgment is required to de-escalate conflict. Because the job involves life-altering decisions, physical custody, and legal liability that cannot be delegated to software, it remains fundamentally resistant to AI substitution despite potential for digital monitoring tools.

rows.title: School and career counselors and advisors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-touch interpersonal work and digital information processing. AI can significantly automate the 'hard' data tasks like analyzing student performance trends, matching aptitudes to career paths, and drafting résumés, but the core functions of emotional support, crisis intervention (reporting abuse), and navigating complex human relationships in a school setting provide a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize data for career planning or academic scheduling, the core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human trust, emotional support, and the ethical responsibility of managing student welfare and crisis intervention. The necessity for a human presence to provide authentic empathy and the high-stakes accountability for reporting abuse or guiding life-altering decisions make this role highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Industrial engineers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial engineering is heavily focused on data analysis, systems design, and process optimization—tasks where AI and machine learning excel. While the role requires physical site visits and human collaboration to understand workflows, the core work product is digital analysis and modeling, making the occupation highly susceptible to significant productivity gains and restructuring through AI-driven automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the industrial engineer's output (process designs, data models, and efficiency reports) is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization, the role requires significant physical site observation and the navigation of unstructured, legacy manufacturing environments. Furthermore, the high degree of systemic accountability—where engineers must sign off on safety-critical systems and manage human-machine integration—creates a strong barrier to full automation, as the legal and ethical liability for physical safety remains a fundamentally human responsibility.

rows.title: Firefighters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of firefighting are intensely physical and require real-time presence in unpredictable, hazardous environments where AI cannot currently operate. While AI may enhance peripheral tasks like incident reporting, equipment maintenance scheduling, and predictive wildfire modeling, the essential work of rescuing victims and extinguishing fires remains a human-centric manual task.
rows.new_rationale: Firefighting is fundamentally a physical, high-stakes intervention in unstructured, volatile environments where the core value is the preservation of life and property through real-world action. While AI can assist in data analysis or logistics, the occupation requires human physical presence, moral authority in life-or-death decision-making, and absolute accountability for outcomes that software cannot legally or ethically assume.

rows.title: Public relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—writing press releases, drafting speeches, creating social media content, and monitoring public sentiment—are digital-first and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level strategy and real-time interpersonal crisis management provide some protection, the vast majority of the daily work product can be generated or significantly optimized by AI, leading to high exposure and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—press releases, social media content, and strategic messaging—is almost entirely digital and increasingly susceptible to AI generation and automated distribution. While the role requires high-level synthesis of public sentiment and organizational strategy, the 'human' necessity remains significant primarily for crisis management, high-stakes relationship building, and the ethical accountability required when representing an organization's reputation to the public.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing circuits, modeling systems, and writing documentation is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While physical testing, site visits, and manufacturing supervision provide a buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in CAD, simulation, and coding significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the knowledge-work components.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and simulation work is increasingly digital and automatable, the role requires high-level synthesis of physical constraints and complex systems that often defy purely algorithmic optimization. Crucially, the high degree of systemic accountability—where engineers must sign off on safety-critical infrastructure and hardware—creates a significant barrier to full AI substitution, as legal and ethical liability for physical failures remains a fundamentally human responsibility.

rows.title: Bakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of a baker are physical and sensory, involving manual dexterity for shaping dough and real-time monitoring of physical products in high-heat environments. While AI can optimize recipes or assist with inventory management and scheduling, the fundamental work of kneading, decorating, and handling equipment remains a physical task resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Baking is fundamentally a physical production process involving the manipulation of raw materials in a real-world environment, which limits agentic output potential despite the existence of industrial automation. While recipes and processes can be digitized, the core value remains the physical creation of food, which requires human oversight for quality control and environmental adjustments that are difficult to fully delegate to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Instructional coordinators
rows.old_rationale: Instructional coordinators perform high-level knowledge work including curriculum development, data analysis, and educational material review, all of which are digital-first tasks highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction for teacher coaching and stakeholder management, the core 'product'—curricula and instructional strategies—can be generated and optimized much faster using generative AI and advanced analytics.
rows.new_rationale: While the design of curriculum and analysis of test data are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, the core value of the role relies heavily on human-to-human mentorship, political navigation within school systems, and the social trust required to influence teacher behavior. The necessity for human presence in coaching and the high-stakes accountability for educational outcomes limit the extent to which an autonomous agent could fully replace the role in a real-world school environment.

rows.title: Postsecondary education administrators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on digital information processing, including reviewing applications, analyzing student data, managing budgets, and maintaining academic records—all tasks where AI can significantly increase productivity or automate routine workflows. While roles like deans and student affairs officers require high-level human judgment and interpersonal relationship management, the underlying administrative and analytical work is highly susceptible to AI integration and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative, data-heavy, and scheduling work is highly susceptible to agentic automation, the role remains deeply anchored in human-to-human interaction, crisis management, and institutional trust. The necessity for human presence in student advising, conflict resolution, and high-level academic governance creates a significant barrier to full AI substitution, despite the high potential for digital output in backend operations.

rows.title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as logistics planning, budgeting, and supply chain optimization, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains. However, the core job also requires physical presence for facility inspections, safety compliance monitoring, and high-stakes interpersonal leadership in unpredictable environments like warehouses and railyards, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While logistics software can optimize routes and inventory, the role requires high-stakes, real-time intervention in physical, unpredictable environments (warehouses, railyards) where human judgment is needed to resolve complex, non-routine disruptions. The necessity for human leadership, conflict resolution, and legal accountability for safety and compliance limits the degree to which an autonomous agent can fully substitute for the manager's role.

rows.title: Speech-language pathologists
rows.old_rationale: Speech-language pathology involves a significant amount of digital-adjacent work, such as analyzing speech patterns, documenting progress, and developing treatment plans, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for swallowing therapy, real-time interpersonal coaching, and high-stakes clinical judgment that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostic data and exercise planning, the core value of this role is the physical, real-time, and empathetic interaction required to rehabilitate speech and swallowing functions, which cannot be delegated to a digital agent. The work involves high-stakes clinical judgment and physical intervention (e.g., swallowing therapy) that requires a human practitioner to hold legal and ethical responsibility for patient outcomes.

rows.title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report writing, and regulatory cross-referencing, the core duties require physical inspections of worksites, manual testing with specialized equipment, and real-time human interaction to conduct safety trainings and emergency investigations.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting can be automated, the core value of this role lies in physical site inspection, real-time hazard mitigation in unstructured environments, and the exercise of professional judgment regarding human safety. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human authority in enforcing regulations and conducting on-site investigations make full agentic substitution unlikely, as the 'buck' for life-altering safety decisions must remain with a human professional.

rows.title: Physician assistants
rows.old_rationale: Physician assistants perform a mix of high-level knowledge work and physical clinical tasks. While AI will significantly augment diagnostic accuracy, treatment planning, and administrative documentation, the role requires physical presence for examinations, surgical procedures, and hands-on treatments like stitching wounds, as well as complex interpersonal counseling that is difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics and data synthesis, the core value of a PA involves physical interventions (stitching, setting bones, physical exams) and high-stakes, real-time decision-making in unpredictable clinical environments. The requirement for human empathy, trust, and the legal/ethical necessity of a human practitioner to hold accountability for life-altering medical outcomes makes full substitution by an autonomous agent highly improbable.

rows.title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital tasks such as logistics planning, vendor research, contract review, and communications, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and productivity gains. However, the core of the job requires physical site inspections, real-time on-site problem solving during events, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and relationship management, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and logistical planning aspects (scheduling, vendor research) are highly susceptible to AI, the core value of the role is rooted in high-stakes, real-time crisis management and the human-centric delivery of social or professional experiences. The need for on-site, physical presence to navigate unpredictable human behavior and venue-specific failures makes the role difficult to fully automate, as clients prioritize the trust, accountability, and emotional labor provided by a human professional.

rows.title: Gambling services workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical hospitality and digital-adjacent information processing. While AI and automation are significantly impacting sports book writers and slot operations through online platforms and automated betting, the core of the job for dealers and managers involves physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time human interaction to ensure a 'pleasant experience' and monitor for physical rule violations.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of these roles—facilitating wagers and managing game flow—is increasingly being replaced by digital interfaces and automated systems, as evidenced by the rise of online gambling. While the physical presence of a dealer or supervisor provides a layer of social trust and atmosphere that maintains a 'human' premium, the actual mechanics of the job are highly procedural and easily translated into digital commands.

rows.title: Construction and building inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical site visits and digital knowledge work. AI and computer vision can significantly automate the 'plans examiner' and report-writing functions, while remote inspection technology is already projected to reduce labor demand. However, the core requirement for physical presence to navigate tight spaces, use manual testing equipment, and verify real-world conditions provides a substantial barrier to full automation.
