rows.new_rationale: While digital reporting and remote inspection tools are increasing, the core value of the role remains physical, site-specific verification in highly unstructured, hazardous environments where AI cannot yet navigate or physically manipulate components. Furthermore, the occupation carries significant legal and public safety liability, requiring a human agent to provide the final, legally binding certification of structural integrity that society currently demands for risk mitigation.

rows.title: Butchers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-time interaction with physical products in a cold, unpredictable environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory tracking and sales forecasting, the primary duties of cutting, deboning, and handling meat are resistant to digital automation and require specialized robotics rather than AI software.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation of biological material, which requires high dexterity and adaptation to the unique, non-uniform characteristics of each carcass, rendering it resistant to purely digital or software-based substitution. While some aspects of inventory management are digital, the physical environment—characterized by cold temperatures, heavy lifting, and the need for real-time safety judgment—remains highly unstructured and physically demanding.

rows.title: Respiratory therapists
rows.old_rationale: Respiratory therapy involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on patient care, such as performing chest physiotherapy, intubating patients, and maintaining equipment in real-time clinical environments. While AI will heavily assist in diagnostic analysis, ventilator setting optimization, and patient monitoring, the core of the job requires physical presence and interpersonal interaction that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: Respiratory therapy is fundamentally a physical, bedside intervention involving high-stakes, real-time physiological management of patients in volatile clinical environments. While AI can assist in monitoring and data analysis, the core value—physical airway clearance, emergency ventilator management, and the human-to-human trust required for patient compliance—cannot be delegated to software, as the physical variability of human anatomy and the legal necessity of human accountability remain absolute.

rows.title: Art directors
rows.old_rationale: Art directors work in a fundamentally digital domain where AI is rapidly advancing in image generation, layout design, and video synthesis. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and client communication, the core work product can be heavily augmented or automated by AI, significantly increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of directors needed to oversee creative projects.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (visual style, design, and layout) is increasingly digital and susceptible to AI generation, the role remains anchored in high-level creative synthesis, client negotiation, and the leadership of human teams. The necessity for human judgment in curating 'authentic' brand narratives and managing complex stakeholder relationships limits full automation, despite the high potential for AI to execute the underlying design artifacts.

rows.title: Fundraisers
rows.old_rationale: Fundraising involves significant digital work including donor research, data analysis, and the creation of promotional messaging, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While major gift solicitation and event management require high-level interpersonal skills and physical presence, the majority of the administrative, analytical, and communication-based tasks are moving toward AI-driven workflows.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the research, messaging, and campaign management can be offloaded to AI, the core value of fundraising is built on high-stakes interpersonal trust, emotional resonance, and the cultivation of long-term human relationships, which AI cannot authentically replicate. The work environment is relatively controlled, but the necessity for a human 'face' to secure major gifts and maintain donor loyalty acts as a significant barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks that AI cannot perform, such as restraining animals, administering anesthesia, performing surgery prep, and providing emergency first aid. However, AI has significant exposure in the diagnostic and administrative aspects of the job, including analyzing lab results, interpreting X-rays, and managing patient records, which may increase individual productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in physical, real-time interaction with unpredictable biological subjects, requiring manual dexterity and physical restraint that software cannot replicate. While diagnostic data processing is automatable, the necessity for human empathy, physical presence during medical procedures, and the high-stakes accountability for animal welfare make this role highly resistant to agentic substitution.

rows.title: Water and wastewater treatment plant and system operators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical labor and digital monitoring, with AI and advanced automation already contributing to a projected 7% decline in employment. While AI can optimize chemical dosing, analyze sensor data, and manage complex system flows, the role still requires physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sampling, and emergency response to mechanical failures.
rows.new_rationale: While monitoring and routine adjustments are increasingly automated, the core value of this role lies in physical intervention, manual overrides during system failures, and maintenance in hazardous, unstructured environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical public health and legal liability associated with water safety, which necessitates human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rows.old_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the output (press releases, campaign strategy, donor communication) is digital and increasingly susceptible to AI generation, the core value of these roles relies heavily on human-to-human trust, high-stakes relationship management, and the 'authenticity' of a human spokesperson. The need for moral authority and the legal/ethical liability associated with public image management and major gift solicitation creates a significant barrier to full automation, as stakeholders require a human 'buck-stops-here' figure to maintain institutional legitimacy.

rows.title: Public safety telecommunicators
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this role—receiving digital inputs (voice, text, video), processing information according to protocols, and dispatching resources—are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the high-stakes nature and need for human empathy provide a temporary buffer, AI's ability to transcribe, translate, and prioritize emergency calls in real-time will significantly restructure the occupation and increase individual worker productivity.
rows.new_rationale: While the core task of dispatching and data entry is highly digitizable and could be executed by AI agents, the role carries extreme systemic accountability and a high requirement for human-to-human emotional regulation during life-or-death crises. The necessity for a human to provide moral authority, empathy, and legal liability in high-stakes emergency triage makes full automation unlikely, even if the technical output potential is high.

rows.title: Rehabilitation counselors
rows.old_rationale: Rehabilitation counseling involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as developing rehabilitation plans, maintaining records, and researching resources, which are highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the profession relies on high-stakes interpersonal interaction, empathy, and physical advocacy for clients with disabilities, creating a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human connection, empathy, and the establishment of trust, which are not substitutable by digital agents. While AI can assist with administrative tasks or resource matching, the primary output—counseling, advocacy, and personalized rehabilitation planning—requires human presence and ethical accountability that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Dietitians and nutritionists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing nutritional data, interpreting research, and creating personalized meal plans are highly digital and data-driven, making them susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. However, the role requires significant interpersonal counseling, empathy, and clinical judgment in medical settings, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate highly accurate, data-driven meal plans and nutritional assessments, the core value of the profession lies in behavioral modification, patient motivation, and the empathetic human-to-human bond required to sustain long-term health changes. Furthermore, the role carries significant clinical liability, as dietary interventions for patients with complex comorbidities require professional judgment and accountability that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Environmental scientists and specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, report writing, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is anchored by a physical component involving fieldwork, site inspections, and laboratory sample analysis that AI cannot currently replicate. AI will likely serve as a powerful tool for modeling environmental impacts and drafting technical documents, increasing individual productivity while leaving the physical data collection and stakeholder relationship management to humans.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize vast environmental datasets and generate reports, the core value often requires physical site assessment in unstructured, volatile environments where sensor data may be incomplete or misleading. Furthermore, the role carries high systemic accountability, as these professionals provide the legal and ethical basis for environmental compliance and public health decisions, which necessitates human professional liability that cannot be fully offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of administrative knowledge work and high-stakes physical presence. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like budgeting, scheduling, and curriculum planning, the core of the job involves physical facility oversight, staff supervision, and building trust-based relationships with parents and children that cannot be replicated by AI.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like budgeting and scheduling have some digital output potential, the core of the role involves managing human dynamics, safety, and emotional development in an unpredictable, high-stakes environment. The necessity for human presence, trust, and legal accountability for the welfare of children makes this role highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Interior designers
rows.old_rationale: Interior design is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD and BIM, which are being rapidly transformed by generative AI capable of creating photorealistic renderings and floor plans from text prompts. While the job requires physical site visits, material inspections, and complex interpersonal coordination with contractors, the core creative and technical drafting tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality 3D visualizations and floor plans (agentic potential), the role requires navigating the high-stakes, unstructured physical reality of construction sites, building codes, and material constraints. The core value remains deeply tied to human-to-human trust, aesthetic negotiation, and the professional liability of ensuring safety and compliance in physical spaces, which limits the extent to which an autonomous agent can fully substitute for the practitioner.

rows.title: Veterinarians
rows.old_rationale: Veterinary work is a hybrid of physical procedures and complex diagnostic knowledge work. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays/ultrasounds) and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical surgery, manual examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication with pet owners that cannot be automated.
rows.new_rationale: Veterinary medicine is fundamentally anchored in physical, real-time manipulation of living organisms in highly unpredictable environments, ranging from sterile clinics to remote, volatile farm settings. While AI can assist in diagnostics, the core value—performing surgery, managing animal welfare, and navigating the emotional/ethical complexities of client communication and euthanasia—requires a human presence that cannot be substituted by digital agents.

rows.title: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital design, with AI and automation already driving a projected decline in dental technician roles. While the physical assembly, polishing, and repair of medical devices require manual dexterity, the 'knowledge' phase of the job—interpreting prescriptions and designing appliances—is rapidly shifting toward AI-integrated CAD/CAM software and 3D printing.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is the fabrication of physical medical devices based on digital or physical specifications, a process already heavily transitioning to CAD/CAM and 3D printing, making it highly susceptible to agentic automation. While the work requires precision, it occurs in highly controlled, predictable laboratory environments where the human role is increasingly limited to oversight of automated systems rather than creative synthesis or essential human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Financial examiners
rows.old_rationale: Financial examiners perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis of balance sheets, loan documentation, and regulatory text. AI is exceptionally well-suited for automating the core tasks of this role, such as identifying anomalies in financial data, summarizing meeting minutes, and checking compliance against complex legal frameworks, though human judgment remains necessary for final risk assessments and high-level management evaluations.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a financial examiner is a digital report and regulatory assessment, which is highly susceptible to automation via AI-driven data analysis and compliance monitoring. However, the role maintains high systemic accountability and a requirement for human judgment in interpreting complex management behaviors and ethical compliance, which limits full delegation to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Actors
rows.old_rationale: While live theater and physical presence on set remain human-centric, the occupation is highly exposed due to AI's ability to generate digital likenesses, synthesize voices, and replace background extras or voiceover artists. The core work product—audio and video—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI, leading to significant industry restructuring and potential displacement in digital media productions.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly generate digital performances (voice and visual synthesis), the core value of acting remains deeply rooted in human authenticity, emotional resonance, and the 'live' connection with an audience, which limits the total substitution of the profession. The high cognitive synthesis score reflects the complex, non-routine nature of character interpretation and creative performance, while the environmental unpredictability score accounts for the varied, often physically demanding, and non-standardized settings in which actors must perform.

rows.title: Surveyors
rows.old_rationale: Surveying is a hybrid occupation that combines significant physical fieldwork with digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS) are rapidly increasing productivity in the mapping and analysis phases, the core requirements of physical site visits, navigating rugged terrain, and providing legally certified human testimony in court create a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While data processing and mapping are increasingly automated, the core value of a surveyor lies in the physical verification of land boundaries in unstructured, often rugged environments where AI cannot yet navigate or resolve physical discrepancies. Furthermore, the occupation is defined by high-stakes legal and professional liability for certifying property lines, necessitating a human agent to hold accountability for the accuracy and legal validity of the work.

rows.title: Craft and fine artists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital creation. While craft artists (potters, glassblowers) and sculptors have low exposure due to the physical nature of their work, digital artists and illustrators face very high exposure as AI can now generate high-quality imagery and complex designs from text prompts. The overall score reflects this split, as AI significantly impacts the commercial and digital segments of the field while leaving traditional physical mediums largely intact.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate digital imagery or designs, the core value of 'fine and craft' art is deeply rooted in human provenance, physical materiality, and the 'aura' of the artist, which AI cannot replicate. The high cognitive synthesis score reflects the complex, non-routine nature of creative vision and artistic expression, while the low agentic potential reflects the fact that the market for fine art and high-end craft specifically demands human-made physical objects rather than digital or automated substitutes.

rows.title: Lodging managers
rows.old_rationale: Lodging managers perform a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate back-office tasks like revenue management, dynamic pricing, and staff scheduling, the core role requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution with guests and staff that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While revenue management and administrative tasks are increasingly digital, the core of the role involves physical site inspection, staff management, and high-touch interpersonal conflict resolution in a dynamic, unpredictable service environment. The 'hospitality' product is fundamentally rooted in human presence and the social contract of service, which limits the degree to which an autonomous agent can replace the manager's role in maintaining guest satisfaction and facility standards.

rows.title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of monitoring data, adjusting power flows, and responding to system abnormalities are increasingly digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and smart-grid optimization. While the high-stakes nature of power infrastructure and the need for physical equipment inspections provide a buffer, the BLS already projects a 10% decline in employment due to technological advances and automated control rooms.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the monitoring and routine adjustment of power grids is increasingly digital and software-driven, the high-stakes nature of energy infrastructure necessitates human oversight for emergency response and complex system integration. The extreme systemic accountability—where errors can lead to catastrophic regional failures or nuclear incidents—creates a hard ceiling on full automation, as society requires a human agent to hold legal and ethical responsibility for life-critical infrastructure decisions.

rows.title: Concierges
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of digital information processing and physical hospitality. While AI can automate core tasks like making reservations, providing local recommendations, and answering emails, the job still requires a physical presence for greeting guests, handling packages, and providing high-touch interpersonal service that is central to the luxury and residential experience.
rows.new_rationale: A large portion of a concierge's tasks—booking, information retrieval, and scheduling—are already being offloaded to digital interfaces and AI agents, resulting in high agentic potential. However, the role retains significant ontological necessity because the 'concierge' experience is fundamentally rooted in human hospitality, personalized social interaction, and the physical presence required to manage local, real-world logistics.

rows.title: Medical transcriptionists
rows.old_rationale: Medical transcription is a purely digital, routine information-processing task that has already been heavily impacted by speech recognition. Modern AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) can now handle complex medical terminology, formatting, and summarization with high accuracy, transforming the human role into a diminishing oversight function or eliminating it entirely.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is a digital document, which is already being generated by speech-to-text software and requires minimal physical interaction. While the role involves verifying accuracy for medical safety, the task is highly structured and increasingly performed by AI, leaving little room for human-specific value beyond final oversight.

rows.title: Entertainment and recreation managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and strategic planning, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the job requires physical presence to inspect facilities, manage real-time emergencies, and provide high-touch interpersonal leadership and customer service in physical environments like parks and fitness centers.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and budgeting have high digital potential, the core value of this role is rooted in physical facility management, real-time crisis response, and the human-centric delivery of 'positive experiences' and hospitality. The necessity for on-site presence, interpersonal leadership, and physical safety oversight in unpredictable, high-traffic environments makes the role highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Archivists, curators, and museum workers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical preservation. AI will significantly automate archival tasks like cataloging, metadata generation, and electronic record management, while also assisting curators with research and exhibit design. However, the physical requirements of handling artifacts, restoring delicate items, and managing in-person public interactions provide a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital cataloging and archival management have high automation potential, the core value of this profession lies in the physical stewardship, restoration, and curation of tangible artifacts, which requires human judgment and physical interaction. The role involves high-level synthesis of historical, scientific, and aesthetic contexts to make decisions about preservation and exhibition, and the 'human element' is essential for the cultural authority and trust required to manage public collections.

rows.title: Environmental engineers
rows.old_rationale: Environmental engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, system design, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is insulated by the necessity for physical site inspections, regulatory compliance monitoring in the field, and the legal accountability associated with Professional Engineer (PE) licensure.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and report generation, the core value of environmental engineering lies in physical site remediation, complex system integration in unpredictable field environments, and the non-delegatable legal liability associated with Professional Engineer (PE) licensure. The necessity for human judgment in high-stakes regulatory compliance and the physical nature of environmental intervention limit the potential for fully autonomous agentic substitution.

rows.title: Agricultural and food scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, research synthesis, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the role is anchored by physical requirements such as field sampling, laboratory experiments, and on-site inspections at farms or processing plants that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and research modeling, the core value of this role involves physical interaction with biological systems (crops, livestock, soil) in highly variable, unstructured environments where real-world intervention is required. The high systemic accountability stems from the critical nature of food safety and environmental regulation, where human oversight remains a legal and ethical necessity for final validation of safety and compliance.

rows.title: Biochemists and biophysicists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on knowledge work, data analysis, and complex modeling, all of which are being revolutionized by AI (e.g., AlphaFold for protein structure prediction). While physical laboratory work and team management provide a buffer, a significant portion of the role involves literature review, grant writing, and data interpretation—tasks where AI can exponentially increase productivity or automate core analytical functions.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can accelerate data analysis and molecular modeling, the core value of this role lies in the physical execution of novel, high-stakes experiments and the synthesis of complex, non-routine scientific strategy. The requirement for human accountability in clinical trials, ethical oversight of genetic research, and the physical manipulation of biological materials in unpredictable laboratory settings ensures that human oversight remains non-delegatable.

rows.title: Set and exhibit designers
rows.old_rationale: The core creative process—including script analysis, conceptual sketching, and CAD modeling—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI and automated design tools that can rapidly produce 3D environments and visual concepts. While the role requires physical-world considerations like material budgets and on-site installation oversight, the shift toward digital production (such as virtual sets and LED volumes) and AI-assisted drafting significantly increases productivity and reduces the total human labor required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase is increasingly digital and susceptible to AI-assisted generation, the core value of the role involves physical installation, site-specific problem solving, and real-world material coordination that cannot be fully automated. The necessity for human collaboration with directors and curators, combined with the need to manage unpredictable physical environments during set construction and installation, limits the potential for a purely agentic replacement.

rows.title: Industrial designers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial design is a predominantly digital occupation where core tasks like sketching, 3D modeling, and rendering are being rapidly transformed by generative AI and advanced CAD automation. While the role requires physical prototyping and cross-functional collaboration, the vast majority of the design process is computer-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and workflow restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (CAD files, renderings, and machine-readable instructions) is highly digital and increasingly automatable, the role requires deep synthesis of engineering constraints, aesthetic trends, and human-centric usability that remains difficult to fully offload to AI. The necessity for human judgment in balancing production costs, safety, and client vision—coupled with the legal and ethical liability for product failure—prevents full delegation to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Materials engineers
rows.old_rationale: Materials engineering is heavily reliant on digital simulation, data analysis, and computational modeling, areas where AI and machine learning are already accelerating material discovery and property prediction. While the role requires physical lab work and manufacturing oversight, a significant portion of the core value—designing alloys, analyzing failures, and writing technical reports—is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly simulate material properties and suggest molecular structures, the final output requires physical validation, testing, and integration into complex, high-stakes manufacturing environments where failure can be catastrophic. The role demands high-level synthesis of multi-variable constraints and carries significant legal and safety liability, making it difficult to fully delegate to an autonomous agent without human oversight and professional certification.

rows.title: Chemical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Chemical engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including process simulation, CAD design, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization. However, the role is anchored by the need for physical presence in laboratories and industrial plants to monitor equipment, ensure safety compliance, and troubleshoot hardware in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: While chemical engineers produce digital designs and simulations, the final output is a physical, high-stakes manufacturing process that requires real-world validation and troubleshooting in volatile industrial environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical legal and safety liability associated with chemical processing, where AI-generated solutions cannot yet replace the professional judgment and ethical responsibility of a licensed human engineer.

rows.title: Microbiologists
rows.old_rationale: Microbiologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, pattern recognition in genetic sequences, and technical reporting—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement to conduct laboratory experiments, maintain biological cultures, and collect field samples provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and report generation are increasingly automated, the core value of a microbiologist lies in physical experimentation, sample handling, and the interpretation of biological phenomena that occur in complex, non-deterministic environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in public health, pharmaceutical safety, and environmental regulation, where human oversight is required to validate findings and assume liability for life-altering scientific conclusions.

rows.title: Petroleum engineers
rows.old_rationale: Petroleum engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, such as reservoir modeling, data analysis, and drilling simulation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity of physical site visits to remote or offshore locations to oversee equipment installation and troubleshoot real-world mechanical issues that AI cannot physically address.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reservoir modeling are increasingly digital, the core value of the role involves physical intervention in highly volatile, high-stakes, and unstructured environments (offshore rigs, deep-earth drilling) where AI cannot yet safely navigate or execute physical operations. The high systemic accountability score reflects the catastrophic environmental and safety risks inherent in extraction, which require human professional liability and ethical oversight that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. AI significantly impacts the data-heavy aspects of the job, such as analyzing GIS data, processing camera trap imagery, and modeling population dynamics, but it cannot replace the physical requirements of collecting biological specimens, handling wild animals, or navigating remote outdoor environments.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and modeling, the core value of this role is rooted in physical, unstructured fieldwork and the direct, tactile interaction with biological systems that cannot be digitized. The necessity for human judgment in high-stakes conservation decisions and the physical unpredictability of remote, wild environments make this occupation highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Court reporters and simultaneous captioners
rows.old_rationale: The core product of this occupation is a verbatim digital transcript, a task where AI speech-to-text and natural language processing are already highly proficient. While legal requirements for human oversight and the need to clarify inaudible speech provide a temporary buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in transcription and real-time captioning creates extreme pressure for automation and massive productivity gains that reduce the need for human workers.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is a digital text transcript, which is already being heavily automated by speech-to-text AI, giving it extremely high agentic potential. While the work requires high accuracy and accountability—particularly in legal settings where the transcript serves as an official, non-delegatable record—the actual task of transcription is increasingly a software-driven process rather than a creative or complex synthesis task.

rows.title: Economists
rows.old_rationale: Economists perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and technical writing. AI is exceptionally proficient at the core tasks of this role, including processing large datasets, identifying statistical patterns, and generating reports, which significantly increases productivity and reduces the number of human workers needed for routine analysis.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an economist—reports, forecasts, and policy recommendations—is almost entirely digital and increasingly generated by automated modeling, leading to high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex, non-linear economic systems, the physical environment is highly controlled, and the primary human necessity lies in the social and political trust required for high-stakes decision-making rather than physical presence.

rows.title: Fire inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence for onsite inspections, evidence collection in debris, and forest patrolling, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI can substantially assist with digital tasks such as reviewing building blueprints, analyzing fire scene data, and automating the documentation of code violations and legal reports.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical site assessment, evidence collection in volatile post-fire environments, and the exercise of legal police powers, which cannot be delegated to software. While AI can assist in documentation and code cross-referencing, the necessity for human judgment in high-stakes legal proceedings, the physical unpredictability of fire scenes, and the requirement for human accountability in public safety make this occupation highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Dancers and choreographers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, athleticism, and real-time human interaction in physical spaces, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist choreographers with music selection or administrative tasks, the fundamental value of the work lies in the human body's movement and live artistic expression.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of dance is rooted in human embodiment, physical presence, and the shared experience between performer and audience, making it highly resistant to digital substitution. While choreographic patterns can be digitized, the artistic synthesis and the 'human-to-human' connection required for performance remain fundamentally tied to human identity and physical reality, which AI cannot authentically replicate.

rows.title: Epidemiologists
rows.old_rationale: Epidemiology is a data-intensive knowledge profession where core tasks like statistical analysis, pattern recognition in large datasets, and literature reviews are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires human judgment for policy-making, community outreach, and field investigations, the shift toward digital health records and automated surveillance systems significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and modeling can be automated, the role requires high-level synthesis of disparate, often messy real-world data and the navigation of complex political and social environments during health crises. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human trust in public health communication—where the 'human' element is required to legitimize policy and manage public fear—limit the extent to which an AI agent can fully substitute for the professional.

rows.title: Podiatrists
rows.old_rationale: Podiatry is a medical specialty that requires significant physical presence for exams, manual procedures, and complex surgeries that AI cannot perform. However, AI will heavily impact the knowledge-based aspects of the role, such as analyzing X-rays, diagnosing conditions from medical history, and automating clinical documentation and administrative tasks.
rows.new_rationale: Podiatry is fundamentally defined by physical, invasive, and surgical interventions on the human body, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While AI can assist in diagnostics, the core value relies on the physical presence, manual dexterity, and the high-stakes legal and ethical accountability of a licensed human practitioner.

rows.title: Atmospheric scientists, including meteorologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing digital data, running computer models, and writing code, all of which are highly susceptible to AI advancement. Machine learning is already revolutionizing weather forecasting by outperforming traditional numerical models in speed and efficiency, potentially reducing the number of human forecasters needed for routine data processing and report generation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession—forecasts, data models, and reports—is inherently digital and increasingly generated by autonomous software, giving it high agentic potential. However, the role remains anchored by high-stakes systemic accountability, particularly in issuing life-saving warnings where human judgment is required to interpret model uncertainty, and by the need for human authority in forensic or broadcast contexts.

rows.title: Aerospace engineering and operations technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as building test facilities, installing instruments, and maintaining hardware, which provides a buffer against AI automation. However, the role also includes substantial digital tasks like recording data, running computer simulations, and calibrating systems—areas where AI is rapidly improving and can significantly enhance productivity or automate data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: While parts of the role involve digital data processing, the core value lies in the physical installation, calibration, and maintenance of high-stakes aerospace hardware in complex, non-standardized environments. The requirement for human accountability in safety-critical aerospace systems, combined with the need for physical dexterity and on-site troubleshooting, makes full agentic substitution highly unlikely.

rows.title: Hydrologists
rows.old_rationale: Hydrologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, computer modeling, and report writing—tasks where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement of field work, such as collecting water samples and measuring streamflow in remote locations, provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While hydrologists produce digital reports and models that are increasingly automated, the core value remains tied to physical, site-specific data collection in unstructured, volatile environments that require human judgment to interpret. Furthermore, the high-stakes nature of water management and environmental safety necessitates human professional liability and ethical oversight that cannot be fully delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Nuclear technicians
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear technicians perform a mix of digital monitoring and physical labor, such as collecting environmental samples and maintaining hardware. While AI can significantly automate the data analysis, radiation calculation, and system monitoring aspects of the role, the requirement for physical presence in hazardous environments and the manual calibration of equipment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While data monitoring can be digitized, the core value of this role involves physical interaction with hazardous, high-stakes environments where real-time, on-site physical intervention is required. The extreme safety risks and regulatory requirements necessitate human accountability and physical presence, making full agentic substitution effectively impossible in the current safety paradigm.

rows.title: Medical dosimetrists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves complex mathematical calculations, image analysis (CT/MRI), and digital treatment planning, all of which are domains where AI and machine learning excel. While there is a minor physical component involving patient positioning and equipment calibration, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that is highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven optimization.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a medical dosimetrist is a digital treatment plan, which is highly susceptible to automation via advanced software and AI-driven optimization algorithms. However, the role maintains high systemic accountability and ontological necessity because the final validation of these plans involves life-critical medical decisions and professional liability that cannot be fully offloaded to a machine without human oversight.

rows.title: Genetic counselors
rows.old_rationale: Genetic counselors perform high-level knowledge work, including analyzing complex DNA data and medical histories, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the role involves providing psychological support and navigating sensitive ethical decisions with patients, requiring a level of empathy and human connection that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize genetic data and identify risks, the core value of the role is the delivery of life-altering, emotionally charged information that requires human empathy, trust, and ethical judgment. The necessity for a human to navigate the psychological impact of genetic findings and bear the professional liability for these high-stakes decisions makes full agentic substitution socially and ethically unacceptable.

rows.title: Agricultural engineers
rows.old_rationale: Agricultural engineering is a hybrid role that combines digital design and data analysis with physical site visits and hardware testing. While AI will significantly automate core tasks like CAD modeling, environmental simulation, and precision farming data analysis, the requirement for physical oversight of construction, equipment testing in the field, and real-world troubleshooting provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural engineers use software for design, their core value lies in applying engineering principles to highly variable, unstructured physical environments like farms and water systems where AI cannot yet manage the physical integration or site-specific troubleshooting. The high systemic accountability score reflects the professional engineering (PE) licensure requirement, where the engineer bears legal and ethical liability for the safety and structural integrity of systems that impact public health and the environment.

rows.title: Home health and personal care aides
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical and interpersonal, involving manual tasks like bathing, dressing, and lifting patients in unpredictable home environments. While AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring vital signs, it cannot replace the essential human presence and physical labor required for personal care.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in physical, in-person care and the provision of human companionship, which cannot be substituted by digital artifacts or software agents. The work environment is highly unstructured and volatile, involving the physical manipulation of clients and navigation of private, unpredictable home settings, necessitating a high degree of human presence and moral/ethical accountability that machines cannot replicate.

rows.title: Cashiers
rows.old_rationale: While the job has physical components like bagging and stocking, the core function of processing transactions is highly susceptible to automation through computer vision and AI-driven self-checkout systems. The BLS already projects a significant decline in employment due to these technological shifts, as AI can increasingly handle age verification, fraud detection, and customer inquiries.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a cashier—processing a transaction—is already largely digitized and increasingly handled by self-checkout software and automated payment systems, making it highly susceptible to full agentic replacement. While the role involves minor physical tasks and customer interaction, these are largely procedural and functional rather than requiring deep human synthesis or high-stakes accountability.

rows.title: Customer service representatives
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—answering questions, processing orders, and resolving complaints—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. Large Language Models and AI agents are already capable of handling complex natural language interactions, and the BLS specifically projects a decline in employment due to automation in this sector.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—resolving inquiries, processing orders, and updating account data—is almost entirely digital and already being replaced by software agents at scale. While human empathy is sometimes required for de-escalating dissatisfied customers, the vast majority of the work is procedural and informational, making it highly susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, sensory judgment (taste and smell), and real-time adaptation in a high-heat, unpredictable kitchen environment. While AI-driven automation is already impacting fast food and routine institutional cooking, high-end restaurant and private household cooking remain protected by the need for complex manual skills and human creativity.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a cook is a physical, consumable product that requires real-time manipulation of raw materials in a dynamic, high-hazard physical environment, which remains largely outside the reach of purely digital agentic execution. While some procedural tasks are automatable, the necessity for physical dexterity, sensory judgment, and the management of unpredictable kitchen variables keeps the agentic potential low, despite the high systemic accountability regarding food safety and public health.

rows.title: General office clerks
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data entry, document formatting, scheduling, and information processing—are almost entirely digital and routine, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While some physical tasks like handling mail or greeting visitors remain, the vast majority of the workload can be absorbed by AI agents and automated software, as reflected in the BLS's projected decline for the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of a general office clerk—data entry, scheduling, filing, and routine communication—are almost entirely digital or information-based, making them highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via software agents. Because the work occurs in highly controlled, predictable office environments and requires minimal complex synthesis or high-stakes accountability, there is very little barrier to full substitution by AI.

rows.title: Janitors and building cleaners
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor in unpredictable and varied environments like schools, hospitals, and construction sites. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like supply ordering or scheduling, the primary work of cleaning, disinfecting, and making physical repairs remains a significant barrier to AI and robotic automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor performed in unstructured, real-world environments that require navigation of complex, non-standardized spaces. While software can assist with scheduling or monitoring, the actual output—sanitation and physical maintenance—cannot be delivered digitally or via remote agentic software, making it highly resistant to substitution by AI agents.

rows.title: Waiters and waitresses
rows.old_rationale: While AI and automation are increasingly handling digital tasks like order-taking and payments via kiosks or mobile apps, the core of the job remains physical and interpersonal. The physical requirements of carrying trays, clearing tables, and providing real-time human hospitality in a dynamic environment create a significant barrier to full AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: While the transactional aspects of ordering and payment are increasingly digitized (lowering the barrier for automation), the core value of the role remains physical service and the social 'hospitality' component, which customers often perceive as a human-centric experience. The environment is physically unstructured and dynamic, requiring real-time navigation and interpersonal management that remains difficult for current autonomous agents to replicate in a way that satisfies the 'service' expectation.

rows.title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and test automation being primary use cases for Large Language Models. While high-level system architecture and complex stakeholder communication provide some insulation, AI is drastically increasing individual productivity, which will likely lead to significant restructuring of entry-level roles and QA functions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is digital code and system architecture, which is inherently compatible with AI execution, leading to a very high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis and carries significant systemic accountability for security and system stability, the environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, minimizing the need for physical human presence.

rows.title: Assemblers and fabricators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation, manual dexterity, and real-time adjustments in a physical environment, which provides a significant buffer against AI. While AI and robotics are increasing efficiency and automating some repetitive tasks, a substantial portion of the work—especially in complex assembly, prototyping, and custom fabrication—still requires human presence and tactile skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation and the assembly of tangible goods, which remains largely outside the scope of purely digital agentic execution. While automation and robotics continue to displace specific tasks, the requirement for physical presence, dexterity in unstructured or semi-structured environments, and the physical integration of components keeps the agentic output potential low.

rows.title: Construction laborers and helpers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor, tool operation, and site preparation in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or specialized robotic equipment control, the vast majority of the work requires human presence, physical strength, and real-time adaptation to physical obstacles.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, volatile environments that require real-time sensory-motor adaptation, which is currently beyond the reach of autonomous agents. While the work involves following instructions, the physical nature of the tasks—such as site preparation, material handling, and assisting tradespeople in hazardous conditions—means there is virtually no digital 'agentic output' that could substitute for the human presence on-site.

rows.title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role—persuasion, relationship management, and negotiation—is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven digital interfaces and automated procurement platforms, which can handle the majority of the transactional and informational output. However, the 'ontological human necessity' remains significant in high-stakes or complex B2B environments where trust, physical presence, and the social capital of a human representative are required to close deals and navigate nuanced client relationships.

rows.title: Bookkeeping, accounting, and auditing clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and involves routine information processing, data entry, and mathematical verification—tasks that AI and automated software already perform with high accuracy. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to technological change, and the shift toward 'analytical and advisory' roles is increasingly being encroached upon by advanced LLMs and specialized financial AI agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is digital financial data and reports, which are already being generated by software and are highly susceptible to full automation. While the role requires accuracy and integrity, the tasks are largely procedural and rule-based, meaning the 'human' element is primarily a placeholder for software that can now perform these functions with higher speed and lower error rates.

rows.title: Material recording clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—tracking data, scheduling, and inventory reporting—are digital information-processing tasks that AI and automated systems excel at. While some physical presence is required to inspect goods or handle packages, the rapid integration of AI-driven logistics software, RFID technology, and computer vision for quality control is already leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is data entry, inventory tracking, and scheduling, which are increasingly handled by integrated ERP systems and automated sensors, making the digital output highly substitutable. While the work occurs in warehouses, the tasks are largely procedural and administrative, requiring minimal complex synthesis or human-to-human emotional labor, rendering the human presence largely functional rather than essential.

rows.title: Grounds maintenance workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier against AI. While autonomous mowers or drones may assist with specific tasks, the majority of the work—such as planting, tree climbing, and complex manual maintenance—requires human dexterity and physical presence that AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of the environment, which is highly resistant to digital substitution; while autonomous robotics may eventually assist, the work requires navigating unstructured, volatile, and high-stakes physical spaces. Because the output is a physical change to a property rather than a digital artifact, the agentic output potential remains extremely low.

rows.title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and digital monitoring. While AI-powered computer vision is rapidly automating the 'surveillance' and 'monitoring' aspects of the job, the core requirements of physical patrolling, detaining suspects, and responding to real-world emergencies provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly handle the 'monitoring' aspect of this role (raising agentic potential), the core value remains physical presence, de-escalation, and the exercise of human judgment in unpredictable, high-stakes physical environments. The necessity for a human to provide legal accountability, physical intervention, and the psychological deterrent effect of a person on-site ensures that AI remains a tool rather than a full substitute for the human guard.

rows.title: Financial clerks
rows.old_rationale: Financial clerks perform routine, digital-first tasks such as data entry, record updating, and basic financial calculations that are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some roles involve customer interaction, the core functions—processing claims, verifying credit, and managing payroll—are increasingly handled by AI-driven software, leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of financial clerks is almost entirely digital—records, transactions, and billing—making them highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via software agents. The work is performed in highly controlled, predictable office environments, and while some roles involve customer interaction, the tasks are largely procedural and functional rather than requiring deep human empathy or moral authority.

rows.title: Receptionists
rows.old_rationale: Many core tasks such as scheduling, answering phones, and data entry are highly susceptible to automation via AI voice agents and chatbots. While the role retains a physical component for greeting and security, the BLS already projects zero growth due to automation, and the digital nature of the administrative workload makes it highly exposed to further restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of a receptionist—scheduling, information routing, and visitor check-in—are highly digitizable and already being replaced by software and automated kiosks. While there is a minor 'human touch' component for first impressions, the tasks are largely procedural and low-complexity, making them prime candidates for end-to-end automation.

rows.title: Childcare workers
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of this role—physical care, safety monitoring, hygiene, and emotional support—require a physical presence and human empathy that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, record-keeping, or parent communication, the fundamental nature of the work is hands-on and occurs in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of childcare is rooted in the physical, emotional, and developmental presence of a human caregiver, which cannot be substituted by digital agents. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the nature of children's behavior and the high-stakes requirement for constant safety monitoring, creating a non-delegatable duty of care that necessitates human accountability.

rows.title: Human resources specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of recruiting, screening, and administrative record-keeping are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime targets for AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction and human judgment for interviewing and employee relations, AI can now handle complex scheduling, initial candidate vetting, and policy inquiries, significantly increasing individual worker productivity and reducing the need for entry-level administrative staff.
rows.new_rationale: Much of the administrative and screening output of HR specialists is digital and increasingly handled by software, leading to high agentic potential for routine tasks. However, the role remains heavily anchored in human-to-human interaction, conflict resolution, and the exercise of moral/legal judgment in sensitive employee relations, which requires a human presence to maintain trust and institutional legitimacy.

rows.title: Market research analysts
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data collection, statistical analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with AI's strengths in processing large datasets and generating insights. While human judgment is still needed for high-level strategy and client relationships, AI can now automate the vast majority of the technical and administrative tasks that define the daily work of an analyst.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a market research analyst—data-driven reports, forecasts, and visual presentations—is entirely digital and increasingly generated by automated pipelines, making it highly susceptible to AI substitution. While the role requires synthesis of complex market variables, the work occurs in a highly controlled, office-based environment where the primary value is the information itself rather than a human-to-human physical interaction.

rows.title: Financial managers
rows.old_rationale: Financial management is a predominantly digital knowledge-work occupation focused on data analysis, reporting, and forecasting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires significant human judgment, strategic advisory, and leadership, the core technical tasks of monitoring financial details and preparing statements are being rapidly transformed by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Financial management involves high-level data synthesis and digital outputs (reports, forecasts, investment strategies) that are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role remains anchored by significant systemic accountability—legal and fiduciary liability for financial health—and the necessity for human judgment in high-stakes, interpersonal advisory roles with top executives, which limits full autonomous replacement.

rows.title: Farmers, ranchers, and other agricultural managers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, equipment maintenance, and real-time management of biological systems that cannot be digitized. However, AI will heavily impact the management side of the job, including crop yield prediction, financial planning, and automated resource allocation, making individual managers significantly more productive and contributing to further farm consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural management involves complex data-driven decision-making that AI can assist with, the core output is the physical production of biological goods in highly unstructured, volatile environments that require real-time physical intervention. Because the primary value is tied to managing living organisms and physical land assets in unpredictable weather and biological conditions, the occupation remains largely resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time mechanical repair in a physical environment, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance software, the actual physical replacement of parts and hands-on troubleshooting remains a human-centric task.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy machinery in unstructured, often cramped environments, which remains highly resistant to digital-only agentic substitution. While diagnostic software assists the process, the final repair requires physical dexterity, force, and real-world troubleshooting that carries significant safety and liability consequences, necessitating human accountability for the vehicle's roadworthiness.

rows.title: Computer and information systems managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving strategic planning, analysis, and technical oversight that AI can significantly augment. While high-level leadership, vendor negotiation, and personnel management provide a buffer of human judgment, the core tasks of assessing IT needs, monitoring security, and directing technical workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the output of this role is largely digital and strategic—making it highly susceptible to AI-driven decision support and automation—the role remains anchored by high-stakes systemic accountability and the need for human-to-human negotiation, leadership, and organizational trust. The complexity of synthesizing business strategy with technical infrastructure requires high-level human judgment, and the legal and ethical liability for system-wide failures prevents full delegation to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Insurance sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing policies, customizing insurance programs, and maintaining records are digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal trust and persuasive sales skills, AI-powered chatbots and recommendation engines are increasingly capable of handling routine inquiries and policy comparisons, significantly increasing individual agent productivity and reducing the need for human intervention in simple transactions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an insurance agent—the selection, customization, and sale of a policy—is almost entirely digital and already being performed by automated platforms (insurtech). While human agents provide a layer of trust and personalized advice, the actual transaction and policy generation are highly susceptible to end-to-end software execution. The role is largely office-based and structured, meaning it lacks the physical unpredictability that would otherwise protect it from AI substitution.

rows.title: Preschool teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical care, emotional support, and real-time social interaction with children under age five, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, curriculum design, and progress reporting, the primary job requires physical presence and human empathy to manage safety and developmental needs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in the physical, emotional, and social development of children, which requires a human presence for trust, empathy, and safety; an AI cannot substitute for the human-to-human bond or the physical supervision required in an unpredictable, high-stakes environment. While lesson planning and record-keeping are digitalizable, the primary 'product'—the nurturing and guidance of young children—is inherently non-agentic and requires constant, real-time human accountability.

rows.title: Construction equipment operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, outdoor environments that require real-time human coordination and manual maintenance. While AI and automation are increasingly integrated into equipment via GPS and computerized controls, the physical nature of the work and the need for on-site safety judgment provide a significant barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of the environment, which cannot be substituted by digital artifacts or software commands. While the work involves technical skill, the primary challenge is navigating highly unstructured, volatile, and high-stakes physical environments where human judgment is required for safety and real-time adaptation to site-specific variables.

rows.title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time troubleshooting in unpredictable physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance scheduling, the actual repair, installation, and physical manipulation of heavy machinery still require human presence and mechanical skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in unstructured, high-stakes industrial environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents or remote software execution. While diagnostic tasks involve cognitive complexity, the physical labor of disassembly, repair, and installation requires human-level dexterity and real-world adaptability that remains far beyond the reach of current or near-future autonomous agents.

rows.title: Postal service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as delivering parcels and navigating outdoor environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI and advanced automation are significantly impacting the sorting and processing components of the job, while the shift toward digital communication reduces overall mail volume and labor demand.
rows.new_rationale: While sorting and processing are increasingly automated, the core value of the occupation remains the physical movement and delivery of tangible goods in unstructured, real-world environments. Because the primary output is the physical relocation of objects rather than digital artifacts, the agentic potential is low, as software cannot execute the 'last mile' of physical delivery without significant robotic infrastructure.

rows.title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, physical stamina, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable environments like construction sites or repair shops. While AI and robotics can automate routine assembly-line welding, the specialized, non-routine tasks and the need for physical presence in varied locations provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.
rows.new_rationale: While welding involves technical precision, the core output is a physical, structural bond in a three-dimensional, often unstructured environment that requires real-time physical adaptation. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical safety implications of structural welds, where human liability remains essential for high-stakes infrastructure and manufacturing integrity.

rows.title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is highly susceptible to automation through autonomous vehicle technology (AI), the job currently requires significant physical presence for tasks like loading luggage, assisting passengers with disabilities, and performing vehicle maintenance. The transition to fully driverless fleets faces regulatory and technical hurdles in complex environments, but the long-term trajectory for this digital-adjacent physical task is one of high potential displacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation—transporting a passenger from point A to point B—is increasingly being executed by autonomous vehicle software, which the market is already accepting as a substitute. While the physical environment of road traffic remains highly unpredictable and requires complex real-time navigation, the 'human' element is largely functional rather than essential, except in specific high-end chauffeur roles where human service is the product. Systemic accountability remains high because the legal and ethical liability for passenger safety in a moving vehicle is a critical barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and information-based, involving market research, data analysis, and content strategy—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While high-level strategic decision-making and stakeholder management provide some insulation, AI can now automate significant portions of campaign planning, creative drafting, and consumer data processing, leading to major restructuring of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—marketing strategies, media buying plans, and campaign creative—is increasingly digital and executable by autonomous agents, leading to high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level synthesis and human-to-human negotiation, the primary value is increasingly derived from data-driven optimization that software can replicate, though human oversight remains necessary for high-stakes brand management and client trust.

rows.title: Administrative services and facilities managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like recordkeeping, supply procurement, and energy pattern analysis, the role requires a physical presence to inspect facilities, oversee manual labor (janitors, repair workers), and manage real-world safety and security compliance.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks can be digitized, the core value of this role lies in physical facilities management, which involves navigating unpredictable, unstructured environments and managing complex, non-routine physical systems. The role requires significant human-to-human interaction for leadership and conflict resolution, and carries high systemic accountability for safety, security, and regulatory compliance that cannot be fully delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Compliance officers
rows.old_rationale: Compliance work is primarily digital and involves analyzing vast amounts of regulatory text, auditing data, and generating reports—all areas where LLMs and automated systems excel. While high-level judgment and interpersonal investigations provide some insulation, the core tasks of monitoring, risk assessment, and documentation are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a compliance officer—reports, policy documentation, and risk assessments—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of parsing complex regulatory frameworks. While the role requires high-level synthesis of legal and operational variables, the primary barrier to full automation is not the complexity of the work, but the requirement for human accountability and legal liability when interpreting and enforcing high-stakes regulatory standards.

rows.title: Dental assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical, hands-on tasks such as sterilizing instruments, assisting in surgeries, and providing direct patient care that requires human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can significantly automate the administrative and digital aspects of the role—such as scheduling, billing, and preliminary X-ray analysis—the majority of the workday is spent in the physical dental operatory where AI cannot currently replace human labor.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person assistance within a highly variable and sensitive environment (the human oral cavity), which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While administrative tasks like scheduling are automatable, the primary functions—patient comfort, physical instrument manipulation, and infection control—require human presence, dexterity, and the ethical responsibility of managing patient safety in a clinical setting.

rows.title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—legal research, document drafting, and information organization—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models.
