While AI is highly effective at analyzing seismic data, GIS mapping, and predictive modeling, the core of the job requires physical fieldwork, sample collection in remote environments, and laboratory testing that AI cannot perform. Consequently, AI will significantly enhance their analytical productivity but cannot replace the essential physical presence and real-world data gathering required for the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While geoscientists produce digital reports and maps, the core value is derived from physical, high-stakes interpretation of unstructured, remote, and volatile environments where AI cannot yet perform the necessary physical sampling or navigate the inherent uncertainty of geological phenomena. The role carries significant systemic accountability, as their assessments often dictate multi-million dollar extraction projects or critical public safety decisions regarding natural hazards, requiring human professional liability that cannot be delegated to an algorithm.

rows.title: Elevator and escalator installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves highly physical, manual labor in unpredictable environments, such as elevator shafts and machine rooms, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can significantly enhance diagnostic troubleshooting and predictive maintenance through computerized control systems, the actual installation, repair, and physical manipulation of heavy mechanical components still require a human presence. AI acts as a sophisticated tool for the technician rather than a replacement for the physical work.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation, installation, and repair of heavy machinery in highly unstructured, hazardous environments, which remains far beyond the reach of current or near-future autonomous agents. Furthermore, the role carries extreme systemic accountability; because these systems transport humans, the legal and ethical liability for safety-critical repairs requires a human agent who can be held responsible for life-altering consequences.

rows.title: Health and safety engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant mix of digital knowledge work and physical presence. While AI can assist in reviewing equipment plans, analyzing accident data, and interpreting complex safety regulations, the core duties require physical inspections of facilities, interviewing employees for investigations, and directing the hands-on installation of safety devices.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize safety data and generate reports, the core value of this role lies in physical site inspections, interpreting unstructured environmental hazards, and navigating complex human dynamics during accident investigations. The high systemic accountability and the need for professional licensure to sign off on safety-critical, life-altering decisions create a significant barrier to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Nuclear medicine technologists
rows.old_rationale: The role requires significant physical presence to prepare and administer radiopharmaceuticals, operate heavy machinery, and physically assist or position patients. While AI will likely automate the digital aspects of the job—such as optimizing image quality, calculating dosages, and streamlining record-keeping—the core clinical and manual tasks remain insulated from full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the imaging output is digital, the core value of the role involves the physical, high-stakes administration of radioactive materials to human patients, which requires real-time physical presence and safety monitoring. The necessity for human empathy, patient stabilization, and the profound legal and ethical liability associated with handling hazardous materials and medical procedures makes this role highly resistant to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and objective data processing. While computer vision and AI can already automate 'calls' (like strike zones or out-of-bounds) with higher accuracy than humans, the role still requires physical presence to manage player conduct, ensure safety, and handle real-time interpersonal disputes on the field.
rows.new_rationale: While automated systems (like Hawk-Eye or VAR) are increasingly capable of executing the 'output' of officiating (ruling on infractions), the role remains highly dependent on human presence for conflict resolution, game management, and the social legitimacy of the competition. The environment is inherently unstructured and volatile, requiring physical presence to manage the flow of play and maintain order among human participants, which limits the total substitution of the role by software alone.

rows.title: Radiation therapists
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence to position patients, operate heavy machinery, and provide empathetic interpersonal care during serious illness. While AI will heavily automate the digital aspects of the job—such as tumor localization, treatment planning, and dosage calculations—the physical and clinical safety requirements create a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the operation of radiation equipment is highly technical, the core value of the role is the physical positioning of patients, real-time monitoring for adverse reactions, and the provision of empathetic human care in a high-stakes medical environment. The requirement for human presence to ensure patient safety, provide emotional support, and accept legal liability for life-altering radiation delivery makes full automation or agentic substitution effectively impossible.

rows.title: Athletes and sports competitors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in the physical world, which AI cannot replicate. While AI is used as a peripheral tool for performance analytics, strategy optimization, and diet planning, it does not replace the athlete's physical labor or the entertainment value of human competition.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical performance and the human-to-human connection inherent in spectator sports; an AI-controlled robot, while technically capable of performing physical tasks, would not be accepted by the market as a substitute for a human athlete. The work occurs in highly unpredictable, high-stakes physical environments where the 'product' is the authentic human struggle, skill, and identity, making it immune to digital substitution.

rows.title: Recreational therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual assistance, and real-time interpersonal interaction through activities like adaptive sports, dance, and community outings. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like patient assessment, treatment planning, and progress documentation, it cannot replace the physical and emotional labor required to lead therapeutic interventions in person.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human connection, empathy, and physical presence, which cannot be substituted by digital artifacts or software agents. While the planning phase involves complex synthesis, the execution requires navigating unpredictable, real-world physical environments and managing the high-stakes emotional and physical well-being of vulnerable patients, necessitating human accountability.

rows.title: Environmental engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report reviewing, and regulatory compliance checks, it cannot physically collect soil and water samples, maintain field equipment, or perform on-site hazardous material disposal. The BLS already notes that automated testing is increasing productivity, which limits job growth even as the core physical tasks remain human-centric.
rows.new_rationale: While data logging and reporting have high digital potential, the core value of this role involves physical interaction with unstructured, hazardous environments (field sampling, equipment maintenance, and site inspection). The requirement for physical presence, manual dexterity in volatile settings, and the legal/ethical accountability for environmental compliance and safety protocols makes full agentic substitution highly unlikely.

rows.title: Geological and hydrologic technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data analysis. While AI and automation (like drones and remote sensors) are increasingly handling data collection and mapping, the role still requires significant physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sample collection, and laboratory testing that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is tied to physical sample collection and equipment maintenance in remote, unstructured, and often hazardous environments where AI agents cannot yet operate autonomously. The high environmental unpredictability and the need for physical interaction with the terrain significantly limit agentic potential, despite the routine nature of some laboratory tasks.

rows.title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work including patient examinations, manual measurements, and the physical fabrication or adjustment of medical devices. While AI and computer-aided design (CAD) can greatly enhance the diagnostic and design phases, the core requirements of real-time human interaction, physical dexterity, and clinical judgment in a healthcare setting provide a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in the design and fabrication of devices, the core value of this role is the physical, high-stakes interaction with a patient's unique anatomy and the empathetic, trust-based clinical judgment required to ensure a device is functional and safe. The physical variability of human bodies and the critical nature of medical liability make this role highly resistant to full agentic substitution, as the 'product' is a successful physical integration between a human body and a custom-fitted device.

rows.title: Anthropologists and archeologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, pattern recognition in artifacts, and report writing, it cannot replace the physical requirements of excavation, site surveying, and in-person ethnographic observation.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and report generation are increasingly automatable, the core value of this profession relies on physical, high-stakes fieldwork in unstructured environments where human judgment is required to identify and preserve fragile, non-digitized artifacts. The necessity for human presence in ethnographic research and the physical, site-specific nature of archaeological excavation limit the potential for full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Survey researchers
rows.old_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of survey research—data collection, statistical analysis, and reporting—is almost entirely digital and increasingly automated by AI-driven data mining and synthetic survey generation. While high-level research design requires cognitive synthesis, the actual execution of surveys and data interpretation is highly susceptible to end-to-end software substitution, as evidenced by the industry's shift toward big data and away from human-led interviews.

rows.title: Models
rows.old_rationale: While runway and live event modeling require physical presence, the majority of modeling work results in digital or print imagery which is highly vulnerable to AI-generated synthetic media. AI can now create hyper-realistic virtual models or 'digital twins' of existing models, allowing brands to showcase clothing and products without physical photo shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human labor in commercial and catalog sectors.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of modeling—a visual representation of a person or product—is increasingly being replaced by high-fidelity synthetic media and AI-generated avatars, which are economically accepted as substitutes in advertising and digital media. While live runway or in-person promotional work retains a requirement for physical presence, the vast majority of the industry's value is derived from digital assets that are highly susceptible to automation.

rows.title: Political scientists
rows.old_rationale: Political science is a purely digital knowledge-work occupation centered on data analysis, research, and writing, all of which are core strengths of generative AI and LLMs. While high-level strategic judgment and interpersonal networking remain human-centric, AI can now automate the collection of public opinion data, the drafting of policy briefs, and the forecasting of political trends, leading to significant productivity gains and a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a political scientist—reports, policy analysis, and trend forecasting—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of synthesizing vast datasets. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis, the environment is largely controlled (office/research settings), and the human necessity is limited primarily to the political legitimacy of the advisor rather than a requirement for physical presence or unique human experience.

rows.title: Desktop publishers
rows.old_rationale: The work product is entirely digital and involves routine information processing, layout design, and basic editing—all areas where AI is highly proficient. Generative AI and automated layout tools can now handle text integration, image adjustment, and formatting with minimal human intervention, leading to a projected decline in the occupation as these tasks are automated or absorbed by other roles using AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a desktop publisher is a digital file (layout), which is highly susceptible to automation via AI-driven design and formatting software. Because the work occurs entirely within controlled digital environments and lacks a requirement for human physical presence or high-stakes ethical liability, it is a prime candidate for full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Sociologists
rows.old_rationale: Sociology is a knowledge-intensive field where core tasks like data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. While qualitative field research and interpersonal interviews provide a buffer, the shift toward digital data collection and AI-driven statistical modeling significantly increases worker productivity and reshapes the research process.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a sociologist—research reports, policy recommendations, and data analysis—is highly digital and increasingly automatable by AI, leading to a high agentic potential. However, the role retains significant human necessity due to the requirement for ethical judgment in human-subject research, the need for interpersonal rapport during qualitative data collection, and the requirement for human authority when presenting sensitive policy recommendations to stakeholders.

rows.title: Geographers
rows.old_rationale: Geographers primarily perform digital knowledge work, including data analysis, GIS modeling, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While some fieldwork exists, the core of the profession involves processing satellite imagery and census data—tasks where AI and machine learning are rapidly becoming more efficient than humans.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a geographer—digital maps, spatial analysis, and data-driven reports—is highly susceptible to automation, as GIS and remote sensing are already software-native domains. While fieldwork introduces some physical unpredictability, the primary value lies in the synthesis of complex datasets, a task increasingly handled by AI agents that can integrate multi-variable economic, environmental, and demographic data more efficiently than humans.

rows.title: Hand laborers and material movers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical, involving the manual movement, cleaning, and packing of heavy materials in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI can optimize logistics, routing, and inventory tracking, the physical labor itself is resistant to AI and requires advanced robotics rather than software-based intelligence to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—moving, cleaning, and packing objects in the real world—which cannot be substituted by digital agents or software commands. While parts of the workflow (like inventory tracking) are digital, the primary product is the physical displacement of matter in unstructured, high-variability environments like warehouses, loading docks, and residential streets, which remains largely resistant to non-robotic automation.

rows.title: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as clearing tables, carrying heavy trays, and cleaning, which are difficult to automate with current robotics. However, AI is increasingly handling digital peripheral tasks like order-taking via kiosks or voice-AI drive-thrus and managing reservations, which reduces the need for human staff in front-of-house roles.
rows.new_rationale: While the ordering and payment components are increasingly digitized, the core value of this occupation remains the physical delivery of goods and the maintenance of a physical environment, which requires human presence and dexterity. The work environment is highly unstructured, involving unpredictable customer interactions and physical hazards, meaning that even if software handles the 'command' aspect, the physical execution remains a significant barrier to full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—scheduling, drafting correspondence, managing databases, and transcribing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of LLMs and AI agents. While some roles require physical presence for visitor greeting or office management, the vast majority of tasks are routine information processing that can be automated or significantly streamlined, leading to major restructuring and reduced headcount per organization.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation—scheduling, document preparation, data entry, and communication—is almost entirely digital and already being transitioned to autonomous software agents. While some roles require human judgment or interpersonal nuance, the vast majority of the tasks are procedural and occur within highly controlled, predictable office environments where AI substitution is highly effective.

rows.title: General maintenance and repair workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnosing equipment issues via computer vision or managing supply orders, but the primary work of repairing buildings and machinery remains a hands-on human requirement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in unstructured, legacy environments (buildings and machinery), which remains highly resistant to digital-only agentic substitution. While diagnostic tasks have some cognitive complexity, the primary requirement is physical dexterity and the ability to navigate unpredictable, high-stakes physical hazards where human liability and on-site judgment are essential.

rows.title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of nursing assistants and orderlies—such as bathing, dressing, and physically transferring patients—are highly manual and require real-time human presence in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like recording vital signs or scheduling, the primary work product is physical care and interpersonal support, which remains largely insulated from AI automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person care, which cannot be digitized or executed via software, resulting in a very low agentic output potential. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the variable physical needs and health states of patients, and the role relies heavily on human empathy, touch, and presence, which are essential to the product of 'care'.

rows.title: Information clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—processing routine data, answering inquiries, and maintaining records—are fundamentally digital and information-based. While some roles like hotel desk clerks or airport agents require a physical presence, the majority of the work involves routine information processing that is increasingly being automated by AI-driven self-service systems, chatbots, and automated data entry tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of information clerks—data entry, record maintenance, and processing standard inquiries—is almost entirely digital and already being replaced by self-service software and automated systems. While some roles involve physical interaction or high-stakes judgment (like court or eligibility clerks), the vast majority of the work is procedural and highly susceptible to end-to-end automation.

rows.title: Management analysts
rows.old_rationale: Management analysts perform high-level knowledge work—including data analysis, report writing, and financial modeling—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills for client interviews and stakeholder management, the core 'back-office' tasks of synthesizing information and developing efficiency recommendations are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a management analyst—strategic reports, process optimization plans, and financial recommendations—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven synthesis. While the job requires high-level cognitive synthesis and human-to-human trust for organizational buy-in, the actual analytical work is increasingly performable by software agents, leaving the human role primarily as a facilitator of change management and a bearer of professional accountability.

rows.title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires presence on a factory floor to handle materials and maintain machinery. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of handling the digital aspects of the job, such as CNC programming, machine monitoring, and quality inspection, which is already contributing to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation and transformation of raw materials into tangible goods, which cannot be replaced by digital artifacts. While the setup and programming components are increasingly automated, the physical environment of a factory floor—involving heavy machinery, material handling, and safety-critical maintenance—remains highly unstructured and resistant to full, autonomous agentic replacement.

rows.title: Carpenters
rows.old_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, material estimation, or business management, the core work of cutting, fitting, and installing materials remains resistant to automation.
rows.new_rationale: Carpentry is fundamentally a physical trade requiring real-world manipulation of materials in highly unstructured, variable environments, making it extremely resistant to digital agentic substitution. While AI can assist with planning and calculations, the core value is the physical installation and structural integrity of buildings, which carries significant legal and safety liability that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Computer support specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of diagnosing technical issues, documenting problems, and guiding users through digital workflows are highly susceptible to AI automation via advanced chatbots and LLM-driven troubleshooting. While some physical hardware setup and complex onsite networking remain, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that AI can increasingly handle, as evidenced by the BLS's projected decline in employment due to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital troubleshooting and system configuration, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via AI agents and self-healing software. While some tasks involve physical hardware interaction, the vast majority of the work is informational and diagnostic, making it a prime candidate for substitution by autonomous systems that can execute commands directly within the digital environment.

rows.title: Material moving machine operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is physical operation of heavy machinery in dynamic environments like warehouses and construction sites, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI-driven computer vision and autonomous navigation are increasingly being integrated into forklifts and conveyor systems, leading to moderate exposure as these technologies augment or replace human operators in structured settings.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation of heavy objects in often unstructured or high-stakes environments, which remains difficult to fully automate despite the digital nature of logistics tracking. While software can assist with routing, the physical execution requires real-time spatial awareness and carries high systemic accountability for safety, making it resistant to pure software-based substitution.

rows.title: Electricians
rows.old_rationale: The core work of an electrician is highly physical, involving manual installation, repair, and navigation of complex, unpredictable physical environments like construction sites and crawl spaces. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, diagnostic calculations, or scheduling, it cannot perform the tactile labor or real-time physical problem-solving required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: Electricians perform highly physical, site-specific work in unstructured environments that require complex manual dexterity and real-time problem solving, making them extremely resistant to digital agentic substitution. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the life-safety implications of electrical work, where improper installation or repair carries severe legal and physical risks that cannot be offloaded to software.

rows.title: Agricultural workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, animal handling, and operating machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier to AI. While AI and robotics are increasingly used for precision agriculture and automated harvesting, the high cost of hardware and the need for manual dexterity in varied conditions mean human workers remain essential for the majority of tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of agricultural work is physical interaction with biological systems in highly unstructured, volatile outdoor environments, which remains largely resistant to pure software-based agentic substitution. While automation can assist with specific tasks, the necessity for real-time physical judgment in unpredictable conditions and the manual labor required for animal care and crop maintenance keep agentic potential low.

rows.title: Medical assistants
rows.old_rationale: Medical assistants perform a hybrid of physical and digital tasks; while AI can significantly automate administrative duties like scheduling, coding, and medical record entry, it cannot perform physical clinical tasks such as drawing blood, administering injections, or taking manual vital signs. The role requires a high degree of real-time human interaction and empathy in a physical clinical setting, which serves as a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks have high automation potential, the core of the role involves physical interaction with patients, such as drawing blood, administering injections, and providing compassionate care, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. The requirement for human presence, empathy, and physical dexterity in a clinical setting creates a high barrier to full automation, despite the routine nature of many individual tasks.

rows.title: Bartenders
rows.old_rationale: The core of bartending involves physical manipulation of objects, manual cleaning, and real-time social interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory management or automated ordering, the primary value of the role lies in human presence, physical service, and the social 'hospitality' aspect which remains difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical act of mixing a drink can be automated, the core value of the occupation is rooted in social hospitality, human interaction, and the 'third place' experience, which AI cannot replicate. The job requires high-stakes, real-time judgment regarding intoxication and legal compliance in an unstructured, often volatile social environment, making human presence and accountability essential.

rows.title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical interaction in a highly unpredictable environment (human hair and skin). While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like appointment scheduling, marketing, or visualizing hairstyles for clients, the physical act of cutting, coloring, and styling hair remains a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is a physical, high-touch service performed on a human body, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software. While the work requires aesthetic judgment and technical skill, the primary product is the human-to-human interaction and the physical transformation of the client, making it highly resistant to AI substitution.

rows.title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing price proposals, monitoring inventory, and reviewing financial reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While high-stakes negotiations and physical site visits provide a buffer, the majority of the work is digital information processing that can be significantly streamlined by AI, leading to higher productivity per worker and a restructuring of the procurement process.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—procurement contracts, inventory orders, and supplier selection—is highly digital and increasingly executable by autonomous agents that can optimize for price, quality, and logistics in real-time. While the role involves some physical site visits and interpersonal negotiation, the primary value is analytical and transactional, making it highly susceptible to AI substitution, though human oversight remains necessary for high-stakes contract liability and complex relationship management.

rows.title: Computer systems analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving data modeling, system design, and technical analysis that can be performed entirely on a computer. AI is highly capable of automating core tasks such as writing technical documentation, generating system diagrams, analyzing cost-benefit data, and assisting with the programming and debugging aspects of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a systems analyst is digital architecture, documentation, and configuration—all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven generation and execution. While the role requires high-level synthesis of business needs and technical constraints, the environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical-world friction that would resist automation.

rows.title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments like crawl spaces and construction sites. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like cost estimation, blueprint analysis via BIM, and scheduling, it cannot perform the essential hands-on installation and repair work.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in highly unstructured, legacy environments where no two repair scenarios are identical, rendering agentic software output largely irrelevant. While planning and diagnostics involve cognitive complexity, the physical execution and the high-stakes legal/safety liability associated with building codes and infrastructure integrity necessitate human presence and accountability.

rows.title: Training and development specialists
rows.old_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—training modules, manuals, and digital instructional content—is highly susceptible to AI generation and automated delivery, leading to a high agentic potential. While the role involves human-centric facilitation and interpersonal collaboration, the actual production of the training materials and the administration of learning systems can be largely offloaded to software, leaving the human element primarily for high-touch coaching or organizational culture building.

rows.title: Animal care and service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are physical and require real-time presence, such as feeding, grooming, cleaning, and physically training animals. AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring animal health data, but it cannot replace the manual labor or the empathetic human-animal interaction required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person interaction with living beings, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software agents. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the volatile nature of animals, and the human-to-human bond (trust, empathy, and communication with pet owners) remains a fundamental, non-substitutable component of the service.

rows.title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnostic software, scheduling, or energy load calculations, it cannot perform the physical installation, repair, or handling of hazardous materials required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in unstructured, often cramped, and hazardous environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While diagnostic software can assist with cognitive tasks, the final execution requires physical dexterity, on-site problem solving, and legal accountability for safety and environmental regulations that a machine cannot currently replicate.

rows.title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including manual dexterity, machine setup, and material handling in a factory environment. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of optimizing CNC programming, interpreting CAD designs, and monitoring machine health, which increases worker productivity and reduces the demand for traditional tool and die making skills.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and programming aspects are increasingly digital, the core value of this occupation remains the physical manipulation of materials and the verification of tolerances in a real-world, hazardous environment. The requirement for physical setup, troubleshooting of mechanical failures, and the high-stakes nature of precision manufacturing (where errors can lead to catastrophic equipment failure) necessitates human oversight that software cannot yet fully replace.

rows.title: Network and computer systems administrators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and involves tasks like configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and 'infrastructure as code' practices. While some physical hardware maintenance remains, the shift toward cloud computing and AI-powered network management tools is already leading to a projected decline in employment as fewer workers can manage larger, more complex systems.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital configuration and system maintenance, which is increasingly handled by Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) and autonomous agents, leading to the projected decline in human employment. While the work requires high-level problem solving and carries significant systemic accountability for organizational uptime and security, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the human element is primarily functional rather than essential for the value of the output.

rows.title: Masonry workers
rows.old_rationale: Masonry is a highly physical trade performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, strength, and real-time sensory feedback. While AI and robotics may assist with blueprint analysis or specialized brick-laying machines in controlled settings, the core tasks of navigating muddy sites, handling heavy materials, and finishing surfaces remain resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Masonry is a fundamentally physical trade requiring real-time interaction with unstructured, volatile environments like construction sites, which are highly resistant to digital substitution. While software can assist with planning, the core value is the physical manipulation of materials and structural integrity, for which the mason bears significant legal and safety liability that cannot be delegated to an AI agent.

rows.title: Physical therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of physical therapy involves manual manipulation, physical assistance, and real-time observation of human movement in a physical environment, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with administrative tasks, medical history review, and generating personalized exercise plans, the high level of physical dexterity and interpersonal empathy required limits its overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: Physical therapy is fundamentally a tactile, in-person service requiring real-time physical manipulation and human-to-human motivation, which cannot be replaced by digital artifacts. While AI can assist in diagnostic planning, the core value lies in the physical interaction, the therapist's professional liability for patient safety during rehabilitation, and the empathetic bond necessary to guide patients through recovery.

rows.title: Logisticians
rows.old_rationale: Logisticians perform high-level knowledge work that is primarily digital, involving data analysis, forecasting, and supply chain optimization—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires human-to-human negotiation and real-world problem-solving for physical disruptions, the core functions of inventory management and route optimization are increasingly being automated or enhanced by sophisticated AI algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: Logisticians primarily produce digital plans, schedules, and procurement commands that are already being integrated into automated supply chain management software, giving them high agentic potential. While the work involves complex problem-solving and coordination, the core output is informational and strategic, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization, though human oversight remains necessary for high-stakes accountability and vendor relationship management.

rows.title: Dental hygienists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision manual dexterity and physical procedures performed on patients in a clinical setting, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like analyzing X-rays or automating patient documentation, the primary work of cleaning teeth and conducting physical exams remains insulated from automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, in-person manipulation of a patient's oral cavity, which requires high-dexterity interaction with a volatile and sensitive biological environment. While AI can assist in diagnostics, the physical service and the necessity of human trust, empathy, and liability for invasive procedures make it highly resistant to digital substitution.

rows.title: Cost estimators
rows.old_rationale: Cost estimation is a data-intensive, digital-first occupation centered on analyzing blueprints, technical documents, and historical databases to predict costs. AI and advanced software are already driving a projected decline in employment by automating routine calculations and simulations, though human oversight remains necessary for complex site-specific variables and high-stakes negotiations.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a cost estimator is a digital document—a bid or budget—which is highly susceptible to automation via specialized software and AI agents that can parse blueprints and databases. While the job requires complex synthesis of variables, the output is purely informational and lacks a requirement for human presence or physical intervention, making it a prime candidate for full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Tutors
rows.old_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of tutoring—instruction, feedback, and practice material—is highly digitizable and increasingly delivered by AI agents, leading to a high agentic potential. However, the role retains a significant ontological necessity score because the human element of mentorship, emotional support, and the ability to adapt to a student's unique psychological or behavioral needs remains a primary value proposition that software struggles to replicate authentically.

rows.title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision physical labor, tactile sensitivity, and real-time human interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral business tasks like scheduling or inventory management, the primary service of cleaning, shaping, and decorating nails remains a manual task that is difficult to automate with current robotics.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is a physical, tactile service performed on a human body, which cannot be replaced by digital output or software execution. While the work environment is relatively controlled, the necessity for human interaction, trust, and the physical manipulation of delicate biological tissue makes it highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Chefs and head cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is highly physical and sensory, requiring manual dexterity, real-time human leadership in a fast-paced kitchen, and the use of taste and smell. While AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks like menu planning, inventory management, and recipe generation, it cannot replace the physical oversight and hands-on food preparation essential to the role.
rows.new_rationale: While menu planning and administrative tasks have high digital potential, the core value of the occupation is the physical creation of food and the real-time management of a high-pressure, unpredictable kitchen environment. The role relies heavily on human sensory judgment, leadership, and the 'human touch' of culinary artistry, which remains a significant barrier to full AI substitution.

rows.title: Automotive body and glass repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks are highly physical, involving manual dexterity, welding, and the use of pneumatic tools in unpredictable repair environments that are difficult to automate. AI's primary impact is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like cost estimation and damage assessment via computer vision, but the physical restoration of vehicles remains a human-centric trade.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy, damaged, and non-standardized objects in unstructured environments, which remains highly resistant to digital agentic substitution. While diagnostic and estimation tasks have some cognitive complexity, the primary barrier to automation is the physical, high-stakes nature of structural repair where human accountability for safety and vehicle integrity is non-delegatable.

rows.title: Computer network architects
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing, documenting, and configuring networks is fundamentally digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization tools. While physical hardware deployment and complex stakeholder management provide a slight buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in automated network topology design, security configuration, and predictive troubleshooting, which will significantly increase individual productivity and restructure the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a network architect is digital infrastructure design and configuration, which is increasingly executable via Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) and autonomous network management agents. While the role requires high-level strategic synthesis and carries significant systemic accountability for organizational uptime and security, the physical environment is highly controlled, and the value is derived from technical performance rather than human-centric interaction.

rows.title: Massage therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of soft tissues and real-time human empathy in a physical setting. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, marketing, and documentation, it cannot replace the tactile skill and physical presence required for massage therapy.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of massage therapy is the physical, tactile, and interpersonal interaction between two human beings, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While the cognitive aspects of assessing a client's needs are complex, the delivery of the service is inherently physical and relies heavily on human empathy, trust, and the social legitimacy of a human practitioner.

rows.title: Phlebotomists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a highly tactile, physical procedure (venipuncture) performed on human patients in unpredictable clinical settings, which is resistant to AI automation. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data entry, sample tracking, and scheduling, the primary value of the role lies in manual dexterity and providing interpersonal emotional support to anxious patients.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is the physical, invasive act of blood collection, which requires high-stakes manual dexterity and real-time adaptation to patient physiology and anxiety. While data entry is digital, the primary output is a physical biological sample, and the necessity for human empathy and physical presence in a clinical setting makes full agentic substitution highly unlikely.

rows.title: Electrical power-line installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving climbing towers, operating heavy machinery, and manual repair in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and diagnostic data analysis, it cannot perform the high-risk physical labor required to install or repair high-voltage infrastructure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, high-stakes manual labor in unstructured, hazardous environments that cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While diagnostic tasks involve cognitive complexity, the execution requires real-time physical navigation of volatile conditions (storms, heights, high-voltage) and carries extreme, non-delegatable legal and safety liability that necessitates human presence and accountability.

rows.title: Sheet metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and on-site installation in unpredictable construction environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI and computer-controlled machinery (CADD, lasers, and automated presses) are increasingly used in the fabrication phase, the majority of the work—including installation, maintenance, and complex physical maneuvering—cannot be performed by digital tools.
rows.new_rationale: While fabrication can be increasingly automated in controlled factory settings, the core of this occupation involves physical installation in highly unstructured, variable, and often cramped construction environments that require real-time physical problem-solving. Because the final product is a physical installation integrated into a unique building structure, it cannot be replaced by a digital agent, and the requirement for on-site physical dexterity and safety-critical manual labor remains high.

rows.title: Computer programmers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of writing, testing, and debugging code are entirely digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models, which are already highly proficient in these domains. The BLS specifically projects a decline in this occupation due to automation and AI, as these tools allow fewer workers to produce the same volume of code and shift higher-level logic to software developers.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a computer programmer is digital code, which is the native medium for AI agents; the market already accepts AI-generated code as a direct substitute for human-written scripts. Because the work occurs entirely within controlled digital environments and lacks a requirement for human physical presence or emotional labor, it faces the highest possible exposure to automation.

rows.title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and manual, involving the handling, feeding, and restraining of live animals in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or diagnostic data entry, it cannot replace the physical presence and manual dexterity required for animal care, sterilization, and surgical assistance.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person care and the safe handling of unpredictable, living animals, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While some tasks are procedural, the high degree of physical risk, the need for real-time, empathetic interaction with animals and owners, and the necessity of human presence in a volatile clinical environment make automation of the primary output impossible.

rows.title: Compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving core tasks like market research, cost analysis, and report generation that align perfectly with AI's strengths. AI can automate the drafting of job descriptions, perform complex compensation benchmarking, and monitor regulatory changes, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for manual data processing.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—compensation structures, benefits administration, and job classification—is almost entirely digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to automation via software agents. While the role requires analytical synthesis and adherence to legal standards, the work occurs in a highly controlled office environment with minimal physical variables, and the human element is largely limited to communication and policy interpretation rather than irreplaceable physical or emotional presence.

rows.title: Advertising sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing sales statistics, preparing media kits, and drafting contracts, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal relationship building, the shift toward programmatic advertising and AI-driven ad placement is already reducing the need for human intermediaries in the sales process, as reflected in the projected employment decline.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—proposals, contracts, and media placement—is increasingly digital and can be executed by programmatic advertising platforms and AI agents that optimize targeting and pricing. While human rapport and persuasion remain relevant for high-value B2B relationships, the technical and administrative components of the job are highly susceptible to automation, as evidenced by the industry's shift toward self-service digital ad platforms.

rows.title: Pest control workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring workers to crawl into tight spaces, set manual traps, and apply chemicals in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like route optimization, pest identification from photos, or automated billing, the primary labor cannot be performed digitally or remotely.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in highly unstructured, variable environments (crawlspaces, attics, varied building structures) that require human dexterity and real-time physical problem-solving. While AI can assist with identification or scheduling, the actual execution of pest management involves significant legal and safety liability regarding the handling of toxic chemicals in occupied spaces, making full automation or remote agentic substitution impractical.

rows.title: Biological technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate data interpretation, report writing, and experimental modeling, the core duties involve manual tasks like setting up equipment, preparing biological samples, and conducting physical experiments that require a human presence in a lab or field environment.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and reporting can be automated, the core of the role involves physical manipulation of biological samples, equipment maintenance, and field collection, which are difficult to fully delegate to software. The requirement for precision in handling hazardous materials and the need for human oversight in experimental setups maintain a moderate level of systemic accountability and physical necessity, despite the high potential for AI to assist in the analytical components of the workflow.

rows.title: Opticians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work such as measuring faces, adjusting frames with tools, and teaching patients how to insert contact lenses. While AI can automate administrative tasks and assist in frame selection via virtual try-on technology, the core requirements of physical fitting, manual repairs, and real-time interpersonal healthcare guidance provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and ordering aspects of the job are highly digitizable, the core value of an optician lies in the physical, in-person fitting, adjustment, and tactile manipulation of eyewear on a human face. The necessity for human presence to ensure physical comfort, provide personalized aesthetic guidance, and manage the delicate, non-standardized physical interaction with a customer's anatomy limits the potential for full agentic substitution.

rows.title: Small engine mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of mechanical components, manual dexterity, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with diagnostic software and technical documentation, it cannot perform the physical labor of disassembling engines, replacing parts, or conducting hands-on repairs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of hardware in unstructured, often cramped or outdoor environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While diagnostic software assists in identifying issues, the final execution—disassembling, repairing, and reassembling mechanical components—requires physical dexterity and real-world problem solving that cannot be replaced by digital artifacts.

rows.title: Medical equipment repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical and manual, requiring dexterity to handle tools, disassemble machinery, and work in tight physical spaces. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software, troubleshooting, and predictive maintenance scheduling, the physical act of repairing, installing, and calibrating hardware remains a human-centric task that cannot be automated by digital AI alone.
rows.new_rationale: While software diagnostics and calibration are increasingly automated, the core value of this role lies in physical, on-site intervention within complex, unpredictable hospital environments where human dexterity and physical presence are non-negotiable. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of the equipment; a machine cannot assume the legal or ethical liability for the failure of a life-support system, necessitating a human technician to certify the safety and functionality of the repair.

rows.title: Insulation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable, confined environments like attics and crawl spaces. AI has minimal impact on the physical installation of materials, though it may slightly assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or material estimation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor performed in highly unstructured, often hazardous, and confined environments that require significant manual dexterity and real-time physical adaptation. Because the output is a physical modification of a building or mechanical system, it is fundamentally incompatible with digital agentic substitution, regardless of advancements in cognitive AI.

rows.title: Community health workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is built on trust, cultural sensitivity, and physical presence within specific communities, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data collection, report writing, and resource matching, the primary duties involve home visits, hands-on advocacy, and real-time interpersonal coaching for vulnerable populations.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human connection, cultural mediation, and the physical presence required for outreach and advocacy, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While AI can assist with data and resource navigation, the 'agentic output' is low because the job's efficacy depends on building trust, empathy, and navigating the unpredictable, unstructured environments of clients' homes and communities.

rows.title: Glaziers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of a glazier is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or precision measurements, the actual installation, cutting, and securing of glass in the physical world remain well beyond current AI and robotic capabilities.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical installation of materials in unstructured, high-stakes environments (heights, active construction sites), which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While the cognitive tasks involve blueprint reading, the physical execution requires real-time adaptation to site-specific variables and carries significant liability for structural integrity and safety, making it highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Surveying and mapping technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS software) significantly enhance data analysis, map creation, and calculation tasks, the role still requires a physical presence to navigate rugged terrain, set markers, and operate equipment in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: While the digital mapping and data processing components are highly susceptible to automation, the role requires significant physical interaction with unstructured, rugged environments that current robotics struggle to navigate autonomously. The 'human in the loop' is currently required primarily for physical site navigation, equipment maintenance in the field, and verifying data integrity in unpredictable conditions, rather than for deep interpersonal or moral necessity.

rows.title: Chemical technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate the compilation of results, report generation, and predictive modeling of chemical properties, the core duties of setting up equipment, preparing physical solutions, and maintaining laboratory instruments require a human presence in a physical environment.
rows.new_rationale: While data reporting and analysis are digital, the core value of the role involves physical manipulation of hazardous materials, equipment maintenance, and troubleshooting in unpredictable manufacturing or lab environments that require physical presence. The high accountability score reflects the critical nature of chemical safety and quality control, where errors can lead to significant industrial or environmental consequences that require human oversight and liability.

rows.title: Hazardous materials removal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor in unpredictable environments, such as scrubbing surfaces, constructing containment areas, and operating heavy machinery. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like record-keeping, regulatory compliance checks, or analyzing sensor data for radiation levels, the primary work requires a physical human presence to navigate complex, hazardous sites.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical, site-specific remediation in highly unstructured and dangerous environments, which is fundamentally incompatible with current or near-future agentic software. While the work follows strict regulatory protocols, the physical manipulation of hazardous materials in unpredictable settings (e.g., disaster sites, confined spaces) requires human dexterity and real-time physical judgment, while the high-stakes nature of handling toxic or radioactive substances imposes significant non-delegatable legal and safety accountability.

rows.title: Optometrists
rows.old_rationale: Optometry is a hybrid of physical clinical procedures and high-level data analysis. AI is highly effective at analyzing retinal scans and diagnostic data to identify diseases, which will significantly augment the diagnostic process; however, the core role requires physical presence for eye exams, minor surgical procedures, and the fitting of corrective lenses, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostic imaging and data analysis, the core value of optometry involves physical patient interaction, manual procedures, and the exercise of clinical judgment that carries significant legal and health-related liability. The requirement for a licensed human to perform physical examinations, manage patient anxiety, and accept professional responsibility for treatment outcomes makes full agentic substitution highly unlikely.

rows.title: Training and development managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—curriculum design, training materials, and performance evaluation—is increasingly digital and can be generated or optimized by AI agents. However, the role retains significant human necessity due to the need for organizational politics, leadership, and the nuanced interpersonal management of staff and trainees, which requires human-to-human trust and authority.

rows.title: Logging workers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving the operation of heavy machinery and manual labor in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI may improve peripheral tasks like log grading through computer vision or optimize harvest planning, the physical requirements of felling and transporting timber provide a strong barrier to AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of logging is the physical extraction and processing of timber in highly unstructured, volatile, and dangerous natural environments, which remains fundamentally outside the reach of software-based agentic output. While some grading and scaling tasks could be digitized, the primary labor involves navigating complex, unpredictable terrain and managing high-stakes physical hazards where human judgment and physical intervention are currently irreplaceable.

rows.title: Health information technologists and medical registrars
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis, organization, and validation of clinical data within computerized systems. AI is exceptionally well-suited for core tasks like data abstraction, classification coding, and generating reports from large datasets, which significantly increases the risk of automation and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital data, reports, and system configurations, which are highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of parsing clinical records and managing databases. While the work requires high accuracy and adherence to privacy regulations, the environment is a controlled, sedentary office setting, and the primary value is the efficient processing and synthesis of information rather than physical presence or human-to-human emotional labor.

rows.title: Environmental science and protection technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this role involves physical fieldwork, such as collecting soil and water samples and inspecting facilities, which AI cannot perform. However, AI will significantly impact the digital aspects of the job, including data analysis, the preparation of reports and charts, and the modeling of environmental impacts.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digitizable, the core value of this role relies on physical, on-site sample collection and inspection in unstructured, hazardous environments that require human judgment to navigate. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical weight of environmental compliance and public health safety, which necessitates human oversight and liability that cannot be fully offloaded to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical labor. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like 3D modeling, data analysis, and cost estimation, the role still requires a substantial physical presence for assembling parts, setting up vehicle crash tests, and operating machinery like lathes and grinders.
rows.new_rationale: While CAD and simulation software handle the digital design aspects, a significant portion of this role involves physical assembly, testing, and troubleshooting of hardware in unpredictable factory or lab environments where AI cannot yet physically intervene. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of mechanical safety and the professional liability involved in validating equipment that must function reliably in the real world.

rows.title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, manual repair, and hands-on craftsmanship that AI cannot replicate. However, AI and automation are significantly impacting the design and appraisal phases through generative CAD tools and automated gemstone grading, contributing to a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: While CAD/CAM and 3D printing allow for digital design, the core value of the occupation remains the physical manipulation of precious materials, which requires high manual dexterity and real-world environmental interaction that software cannot currently replicate. The ontological necessity is significant because jewelry often carries sentimental, artisanal, or luxury value that relies on the 'human-made' narrative, though the appraisal and certification aspects are increasingly susceptible to automated verification.

rows.title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical maintenance, manual repairs, and hands-on troubleshooting of heavy mechanical equipment in unpredictable physical environments. While AI and automated control systems can enhance monitoring and data logging, they cannot perform the physical tasks of overhauling valves, lubricating parts, or crawling inside boilers for inspections.
rows.new_rationale: While monitoring and control systems are increasingly digitized, the core value of this role lies in physical maintenance, manual repair, and navigating complex, often hazardous, physical environments that require human dexterity and on-site troubleshooting. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of boiler and pressure vessel safety, where human oversight is legally and ethically mandated to prevent catastrophic failure in high-stakes environments like hospitals.

rows.title: Semiconductor processing technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, requiring presence in cleanrooms to handle wafers, maintain equipment, and perform manual inspections. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of monitoring process data, optimizing machine parameters, and performing high-precision quality control, which automates the information-processing and monitoring aspects of the job.
rows.new_rationale: While the work involves digital interfaces, the core value is the physical manipulation of delicate silicon wafers and the maintenance of a high-stakes, sterile physical environment that requires human dexterity and on-site troubleshooting. The agentic potential is low because the job is fundamentally about overseeing and physically intervening in a complex, high-precision manufacturing process where human presence is required for quality control and equipment repair, rather than producing a digital artifact.

rows.title: Credit counselors
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of credit counseling—analyzing financial data, creating budgets, and explaining debt management options—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal skills and emotional support for clients in financial distress, AI can already perform the complex calculations and personalized recommendation generation that constitute the bulk of the technical work.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of credit counseling—debt management plans, budget analysis, and financial communication—is highly digital and procedural, making it a prime candidate for end-to-end automation via software agents. While the role requires empathy and interpersonal rapport to manage client stress and behavioral change, the technical financial advice and negotiation tasks are increasingly commoditized by algorithmic systems, limiting the necessity for a human agent to those seeking emotional support or complex, non-standardized advocacy.

rows.title: Solar photovoltaic installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual labor, climbing roofs, and using hand tools in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like system design optimization or site measurements via computer vision, the actual installation and maintenance require human dexterity and physical presence that current and near-future AI/robotics cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, variable environments (rooftops, attics, crawlspaces) that require real-time physical navigation and manual dexterity, which software agents cannot perform. While the planning phase involves some cognitive synthesis, the primary output is a physical installation that must adhere to strict safety and building codes, necessitating human accountability for life-safety and fire-risk outcomes.

rows.title: Exercise physiologists
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical presence, including conducting stress tests, measuring vital signs with medical equipment, and providing hands-on rehabilitative care. While AI can highly automate the digital aspects of the job—such as analyzing medical history, processing biometric data, and generating personalized exercise regimens—the core requirement for real-time human interaction, physical assessment, and clinical supervision in a medical setting limits its total exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate exercise plans and analyze biometric data, the core value of this role lies in the physical, real-time monitoring of patients with chronic conditions and the human-centric delivery of care. The high systemic accountability for patient safety during physical exertion and the necessity of human empathy and physical presence in a clinical setting make full agentic substitution highly unlikely.

rows.title: Fishing and hunting workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and take place in unpredictable outdoor environments, requiring manual labor, mechanical repair, and real-time physical coordination. AI's impact is limited to peripheral enhancements in fish-finding sonar, GPS navigation, and regulatory data processing, but it cannot replace the physical act of hauling nets, trapping animals, or maintaining vessels.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical extraction of biological resources from highly volatile, unstructured, and hazardous natural environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of autonomous agents. While navigation and fish-finding can be digitized, the physical labor of handling gear, managing vessels in storms, and navigating unpredictable terrain makes the output inherently physical and resistant to software-based substitution.

rows.title: Compensation and benefits managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and data-driven, involving the analysis of wage trends, benefits structures, and regulatory compliance—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires high-level human judgment for vendor negotiations and strategic decision-making, AI can automate significant portions of the data analysis, reporting, and routine employee communication, leading to higher productivity and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—compensation structures, policy design, and budget management—is largely digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. While the role requires high-level synthesis and carries significant legal and fiduciary accountability, the physical environment is entirely controlled, and the human-to-human component is increasingly being replaced by self-service portals and automated HR systems.

rows.title: Audiologists
rows.old_rationale: Audiology is a clinical healthcare profession that requires physical presence for examinations, wax removal, and fitting hardware like hearing aids or cochlear implants. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy, signal processing in hearing devices, and automated testing, the core of the job involves interpersonal counseling, physical patient interaction, and complex clinical judgment that cannot be fully digitized.
